{
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/geraldappel/percentagepriceoscillator/percentagepriceoscillator.go"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/geraldappel/percentagepriceoscillator/percentagepriceoscillator.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package percentagepriceoscillator
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"math"
6: 	"sync"
7: 
8: 	"zpano/entities"
9: 	"zpano/indicators/common/exponentialmovingaverage"
10: 	"zpano/indicators/common/simplemovingaverage"
11: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
12: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
13: )
14: 
15: // lineUpdater is an interface for indicators that accept a single scalar and return a value.
16: type lineUpdater interface {
17: 	Update(float64) float64
18: 	IsPrimed() bool
19: }
20: 
21: // PercentagePriceOscillator is Gerald Appel's Percentage Price Oscillator (PPO).
22: //
23: // PPO is calculated by subtracting the slower moving average from the faster moving
24: // average and then dividing the result by the slower moving average, expressed as a percentage.
25: //
26: //	PPO = 100 * (fast_ma - slow_ma) / slow_ma
27: //
28: // Reference:
29: //
30: // Appel, Gerald (2005). Technical Analysis: Power Tools for Active Investors.
31: type PercentagePriceOscillator struct {
32: 	mu sync.RWMutex
33: 	core.LineIndicator
34: 	fastMA lineUpdater
35: 	slowMA lineUpdater
36: 	value  float64
37: 	primed bool
38: }
39: 
40: // NewPercentagePriceOscillator returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
41: func NewPercentagePriceOscillator(p *PercentagePriceOscillatorParams) (*PercentagePriceOscillator, error) {
42: 	const (
43: 		invalid   = "invalid percentage price oscillator parameters"
44: 		fmts      = "%s: %s"
45: 		fmtw      = "%s: %w"
46: 		minLength = 2
47: 	)
48: 
49: 	if p.FastLength < minLength {
50: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "fast length should be greater than 1")
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	if p.SlowLength < minLength {
54: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "slow length should be greater than 1")
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	bc := p.BarComponent
58: 	if bc == 0 {
59: 		bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	qc := p.QuoteComponent
63: 	if qc == 0 {
64: 		qc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	tc := p.TradeComponent
68: 	if tc == 0 {
69: 		tc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	var (
73: 		err       error
74: 		barFunc   entities.BarFunc
75: 		quoteFunc entities.QuoteFunc
76: 		tradeFunc entities.TradeFunc
77: 	)
78: 
79: 	if barFunc, err = entities.BarComponentFunc(bc); err != nil {
80: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	if quoteFunc, err = entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc); err != nil {
84: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	if tradeFunc, err = entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc); err != nil {
88: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	var fastMA, slowMA lineUpdater
92: 
93: 	var maLabel string
94: 
95: 	switch p.MovingAverageType {
96: 	case EMA:
97: 		maLabel = "EMA"
98: 
99: 		fast, e := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(
100: 			&exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams{
101: 				Length:         p.FastLength,
102: 				FirstIsAverage: p.FirstIsAverage,
103: 			})
104: 		if e != nil {
105: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
106: 		}
107: 
108: 		slow, e := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(
109: 			&exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams{
110: 				Length:         p.SlowLength,
111: 				FirstIsAverage: p.FirstIsAverage,
112: 			})
113: 		if e != nil {
114: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
115: 		}
116: 
117: 		fastMA = fast
118: 		slowMA = slow
119: 	default:
120: 		maLabel = "SMA"
121: 
122: 		fast, e := simplemovingaverage.NewSimpleMovingAverage(
123: 			&simplemovingaverage.SimpleMovingAverageParams{Length: p.FastLength})
124: 		if e != nil {
125: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
126: 		}
127: 
128: 		slow, e := simplemovingaverage.NewSimpleMovingAverage(
129: 			&simplemovingaverage.SimpleMovingAverageParams{Length: p.SlowLength})
130: 		if e != nil {
131: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
132: 		}
133: 
134: 		fastMA = fast
135: 		slowMA = slow
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	mnemonic := fmt.Sprintf("ppo(%s%d/%s%d%s)", maLabel, p.FastLength, maLabel, p.SlowLength,
139: 		core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc))
140: 	desc := "Percentage Price Oscillator " + mnemonic
141: 
142: 	ppo := &PercentagePriceOscillator{
143: 		fastMA: fastMA,
144: 		slowMA: slowMA,
145: 		value:  math.NaN(),
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	ppo.LineIndicator = core.NewLineIndicator(mnemonic, desc, barFunc, quoteFunc, tradeFunc, ppo.Update)
149: 
150: 	return ppo, nil
151: }
152: 
153: // IsPrimed indicates whether the indicator is primed.
154: func (p *PercentagePriceOscillator) IsPrimed() bool {
155: 	p.mu.RLock()
156: 	defer p.mu.RUnlock()
157: 
158: 	return p.primed
159: }
160: 
161: // Metadata describes the output data of the indicator.
162: func (p *PercentagePriceOscillator) Metadata() core.Metadata {
163: 	return core.Metadata{
164: 		Type:        core.PercentagePriceOscillator,
165: 		Mnemonic:    p.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
166: 		Description: p.LineIndicator.Description,
167: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
168: 			{
169: 				Kind:        int(PercentagePriceOscillatorValue),
170: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
171: 				Mnemonic:    p.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
172: 				Description: p.LineIndicator.Description,
173: 			},
174: 		},
175: 	}
176: }
177: 
178: // Update updates the value of the indicator given the next sample.
179: func (p *PercentagePriceOscillator) Update(sample float64) float64 {
180: 	const epsilon = 1e-8
181: 
182: 	if math.IsNaN(sample) {
183: 		return sample
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	p.mu.Lock()
187: 	defer p.mu.Unlock()
188: 
189: 	slow := p.slowMA.Update(sample)
190: 	fast := p.fastMA.Update(sample)
191: 	p.primed = p.slowMA.IsPrimed() && p.fastMA.IsPrimed()
192: 
193: 	if math.IsNaN(fast) || math.IsNaN(slow) {
194: 		p.value = math.NaN()
195: 
196: 		return p.value
197: 	}
198: 
199: 	if math.Abs(slow) < epsilon {
200: 		p.value = 0
201: 	} else {
202: 		p.value = 100 * (fast - slow) / slow
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	return p.value
206: }

(End of file - total 206 lines)
</content>
```

<type>file</type>
<content>1: //nolint:dupl
2: package percentagepriceoscillator
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"bytes"
6: 	"fmt"
7: )
8: 
9: // PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput describes the outputs of the indicator.
10: type PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput int
11: 
12: const (
13: 	// The scalar value of the percentage price oscillator.
14: 	PercentagePriceOscillatorValue PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput = iota + 1
15: 	percentagePriceOscillatorLast
16: )
17: 
18: const (
19: 	percentagePriceOscillatorOutputValue   = "value"
20: 	percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown = "unknown"
21: )
22: 
23: // String implements the Stringer interface.
24: func (o PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput) String() string {
25: 	switch o {
26: 	case PercentagePriceOscillatorValue:
27: 		return percentagePriceOscillatorOutputValue
28: 	default:
29: 		return percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown
30: 	}
31: }
32: 
33: // IsKnown determines if this output is known.
34: func (o PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput) IsKnown() bool {
35: 	return o >= PercentagePriceOscillatorValue && o < percentagePriceOscillatorLast
36: }
37: 
38: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
39: func (o PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
40: 	const (
41: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown percentage price oscillator output"
42: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
43: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
44: 	)
45: 
46: 	s := o.String()
47: 	if s == percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown {
48: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
52: 	b = append(b, dqc)
53: 	b = append(b, s...)
54: 	b = append(b, dqc)
55: 
56: 	return b, nil
57: }
58: 
59: // UnmarshalJSON implements the Unmarshaler interface.
60: func (o *PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
61: 	const (
62: 		errFmt = "cannot unmarshal '%s': unknown percentage price oscillator output"
63: 		dqs    = "\"" // Double quote string.
64: 	)
65: 
66: 	d := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)
67: 	s := string(d)
68: 
69: 	switch s {
70: 	case percentagePriceOscillatorOutputValue:
71: 		*o = PercentagePriceOscillatorValue
72: 	default:
73: 		return fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	return nil
77: }

(End of file - total 77 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>1: //nolint:testpackage,dupl
2: package percentagepriceoscillator
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"testing"
6: )
7: 
8: func TestOutputString(t *testing.T) {
9: 	t.Parallel()
10: 
11: 	tests := []struct {
12: 		o    PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput
13: 		text string
14: 	}{
15: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorValue, percentagePriceOscillatorOutputValue},
16: 		{percentagePriceOscillatorLast, percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown},
17: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(0), percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown},
18: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(9999), percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown},
19: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(-9999), percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown},
20: 	}
21: 
22: 	for _, tt := range tests {
23: 		exp := tt.text
24: 		act := tt.o.String()
25: 
26: 		if exp != act {
27: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.String(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
28: 		}
29: 	}
30: }
31: 
32: func TestOutputIsKnown(t *testing.T) {
33: 	t.Parallel()
34: 
35: 	tests := []struct {
36: 		o       PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput
37: 		boolean bool
38: 	}{
39: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorValue, true},
40: 		{percentagePriceOscillatorLast, false},
41: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(0), false},
42: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(9999), false},
43: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(-9999), false},
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	for _, tt := range tests {
47: 		exp := tt.boolean
48: 		act := tt.o.IsKnown()
49: 
50: 		if exp != act {
51: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.IsKnown(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
52: 		}
53: 	}
54: }
55: 
56: func TestOutputMarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {
57: 	t.Parallel()
58: 
59: 	const dqs = "\""
60: 
61: 	var nilstr string
62: 	tests := []struct {
63: 		o         PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput
64: 		json      string
65: 		succeeded bool
66: 	}{
67: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorValue, dqs + percentagePriceOscillatorOutputValue + dqs, true},
68: 		{percentagePriceOscillatorLast, nilstr, false},
69: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(9999), nilstr, false},
70: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(-9999), nilstr, false},
71: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput(0), nilstr, false},
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	for _, tt := range tests {
75: 		exp := tt.json
76: 		bs, err := tt.o.MarshalJSON()
77: 
78: 		if err != nil && tt.succeeded {
79: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected success '%v', got error %v", tt.o, exp, err)
80: 			continue
81: 		}
82: 
83: 		if err == nil && !tt.succeeded {
84: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected error, got success", tt.o)
85: 			continue
86: 		}
87: 
88: 		act := string(bs)
89: 		if exp != act {
90: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
91: 		}
92: 	}
93: }
94: 
95: func TestOutputUnmarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {
96: 	t.Parallel()
97: 
98: 	const dqs = "\""
99: 
100: 	var zero PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput
101: 	tests := []struct {
102: 		o         PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput
103: 		json      string
104: 		succeeded bool
105: 	}{
106: 		{PercentagePriceOscillatorValue, dqs + percentagePriceOscillatorOutputValue + dqs, true},
107: 		{zero, dqs + percentagePriceOscillatorOutputUnknown + dqs, false},
108: 		{zero, dqs + "foobar" + dqs, false},
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	for _, tt := range tests {
112: 		exp := tt.o
113: 		bs := []byte(tt.json)
114: 
115: 		var o PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput
116: 
117: 		err := o.UnmarshalJSON(bs)
118: 		if err != nil && tt.succeeded {
119: 			t.Errorf("UnmarshalJSON('%v'): expected success '%v', got error %v", tt.json, exp, err)
120: 			continue
121: 		}
122: 
123: 		if err == nil && !tt.succeeded {
124: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected error, got success", tt.json)
125: 			continue
126: 		}
127: 
128: 		if exp != o {
129: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.json, exp, o)
130: 		}
131: 	}
132: }

(End of file - total 132 lines)
</content>
```

419: 
420:         [TestMethod]
421:         public void BollingerBands_Metadata_ValuesAreCorrect()
422:         {
423:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
424:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
425:             var metadata = target.Metadata;
426: 
427:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorType.BollingerBands, metadata.IndicatorType);
428:             Assert.IsNotNull(metadata.Outputs);
429:             Assert.AreEqual(7, metadata.Outputs.Length);
430: 
431:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.MiddleMovingAverageValue, metadata.Outputs[0].Kind);
432:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[0].Type);
433: 
434:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.StandardDeviationValue, metadata.Outputs[1].Kind);
435:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[1].Type);
436: 
437:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.LowerBandValue, metadata.Outputs[2].Kind);
438:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[2].Type);
439: 
440:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.UpperBandValue, metadata.Outputs[3].Kind);
441:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[3].Type);
442: 
443:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.PercentBandValue, metadata.Outputs[4].Kind);
444:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[4].Type);
445: 
446:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.BandWidthValue, metadata.Outputs[5].Kind);
447:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[5].Type);
448: 
449:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.LowerUpperBand, metadata.Outputs[6].Kind);
450:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Band, metadata.Outputs[6].Type);
451:         }
452: 
453:         [TestMethod]
454:         public void BollingerBands_Metadata_SampleStDev_NameAndDescriptionAreCorrect()
455:         {
456:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters { StandardDeviationIsUnbiased = true };
457:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
458:             var metadata = target.Metadata;
459: 
460:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorType.BollingerBands, metadata.IndicatorType);
461:             Assert.IsNotNull(metadata.Outputs);
462:             Assert.AreEqual(7, metadata.Outputs.Length);
463: 
464:             Assert.AreEqual("ma-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Name);
465:             Assert.AreEqual("Moving Average used by Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Description);
466: 
467:             Assert.AreEqual("stdev-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Name);
468:             Assert.AreEqual("Standard Deviation of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Description);
469: 
470:             Assert.AreEqual("lo-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Name);
471:             Assert.AreEqual("Lower Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Description);
472: 
473:             Assert.AreEqual("up-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Name);
474:             Assert.AreEqual("Upper Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Description);
475: 
476:             Assert.AreEqual("%b(c)-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Name);
477:             Assert.AreEqual("%B(c) of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Description);
478: 
479:             Assert.AreEqual("bw-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Name);
480:             Assert.AreEqual("Band Width of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Description);
481: 
482:             Assert.AreEqual("bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Name);
483:             Assert.AreEqual("Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Description);
484:         }
485: 
486:         [TestMethod]
487:         public void BollingerBands_Metadata_PopulationStDev_NameAndDescriptionAreCorrect()
488:         {
489:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
490:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
491:             var metadata = target.Metadata;
492: 
493:             Assert.AreEqual("ma-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Name);
494:             Assert.AreEqual("Moving Average used by Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Description);
495: 
496:             Assert.AreEqual("stdev-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Name);
497:             Assert.AreEqual("Standard Deviation of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Description);
498: 
499:             Assert.AreEqual("lo-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Name);
500:             Assert.AreEqual("Lower Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Description);
501: 
502:             Assert.AreEqual("up-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Name);
503:             Assert.AreEqual("Upper Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Description);
504: 
505:             Assert.AreEqual("%b(c)-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Name);
506:             Assert.AreEqual("%B(c) of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Description);
507: 
508:             Assert.AreEqual("bw-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Name);
509:             Assert.AreEqual("Band Width of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Description);
510: 
511:             Assert.AreEqual("bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Name);
512:             Assert.AreEqual("Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Description);
513:         }

832:         [TestMethod]
833:         public void BollingerBands_IsPrimed_FunctionsCorrectly()
834:         {
835:             var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, 1d);
836:             int count = ClosingPrice.Length;
837: 
838:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
839:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
840: 
841:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
842:             {
843:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
844:                 target.Update(scalar);
845:                 Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
846:             }
847: 
848:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
849:             {
850:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
851:                 target.Update(scalar);
852:                 Assert.IsTrue(target.IsPrimed);
853:             }
854: 
855:             target.Reset();
856: 
857:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
858:             {
859:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
860:                 target.Update(scalar);
861:                 Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
862:             }
863: 
864:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
865:             {
866:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
867:                 target.Update(scalar);
868:                 Assert.IsTrue(target.IsPrimed);
869:             }
870:         }

24: 
25: const (
26: 	aroonOutputUp      = "up"
27: 	aroonOutputDown    = "down"
28: 	aroonOutputOsc     = "osc"
29: 	aroonOutputUnknown = "unknown"
30: )
31: 
32: // String implements the Stringer interface.
33: func (o AroonOutput) String() string {
34: 	switch o {
35: 	case AroonUp:
36: 		return aroonOutputUp
37: 	case AroonDown:
38: 		return aroonOutputDown
39: 	case AroonOsc:
40: 		return aroonOutputOsc
41: 	default:
42: 		return aroonOutputUnknown
43: 	}
44: }
45: 
46: // IsKnown determines if this output is known.
47: func (o AroonOutput) IsKnown() bool {
48: 	return o >= AroonUp && o < aroonLast
49: }
50: 
51: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
52: func (o AroonOutput) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
53: 	const (
54: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown aroon output"
55: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
56: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
57: 	)
58: 
59: 	s := o.String()
60: 	if s == aroonOutputUnknown {
61: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
65: 	b = append(b, dqc)
66: 	b = append(b, s...)
67: 	b = append(b, dqc)
68: 
69: 	return b, nil
70: }
71: 
72: // UnmarshalJSON implements the Unmarshaler interface.
73: func (o *AroonOutput) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
74: 	const (
75: 		errFmt = "cannot unmarshal '%s': unknown aroon output"
76: 		dqs    = "\"" // Double quote string.
77: 	)
78: 
79: 	d := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)
80: 	s := string(d)
81: 
82: 	switch s {
83: 	case aroonOutputUp:
84: 		*o = AroonUp
85: 	case aroonOutputDown:
86: 		*o = AroonDown
87: 	case aroonOutputOsc:
88: 		*o = AroonOsc
89: 	default:
90: 		return fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	return nil
94: }

(End of file - total 94 lines)
</content>

**Tool: read**

```

**Output:**
```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/tusharchande/aroon/aroon.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package aroon
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"math"
6: 	"sync"
7: 
8: 	"zpano/entities"
9: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
10: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
11: )
12: 
13: // Aroon is Tushar Chande's Aroon indicator.
14: //
15: // The Aroon indicator measures the number of periods since the highest high
16: // and lowest low within a lookback window. It produces three outputs:
17: //   - AroonUp: 100 * (Length - periods since highest high) / Length
18: //   - AroonDown: 100 * (Length - periods since lowest low) / Length
19: //   - AroonOsc: AroonUp - AroonDown
20: //
21: // The indicator requires bar data (high, low). For scalar, quote, and
22: // trade updates, the single value substitutes for both.
23: //
24: // Reference:
25: //
26: // Chande, Tushar S. (1995). "The New Technical Trader". John Wiley & Sons.
27: type Aroon struct {
28: 	mu sync.RWMutex
29: 
30: 	length int
31: 	factor float64
32: 
33: 	// Circular buffers for high and low values (size = length+1).
34: 	highBuf []float64
35: 	lowBuf  []float64
36: 	bufIdx  int
37: 	count   int
38: 
39: 	// Tracked indices of highest high and lowest low (absolute indices).
40: 	highestIdx int
41: 	lowestIdx  int
42: 
43: 	up     float64
44: 	down   float64
45: 	osc    float64
46: 	primed bool
47: 
48: 	mnemonic string
49: }
50: 
51: // NewAroon returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
52: func NewAroon(p *AroonParams) (*Aroon, error) {
53: 	const (
54: 		invalid   = "invalid aroon parameters"
55: 		fmts      = "%s: %s"
56: 		minLength = 2
57: 	)
58: 
59: 	if p.Length < minLength {
60: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "length should be greater than 1")
61: 	}
62: 
63: 	mnemonic := fmt.Sprintf("aroon(%d)", p.Length)
64: 	windowSize := p.Length + 1
65: 
66: 	return &Aroon{
67: 		length:   p.Length,
68: 		factor:   100.0 / float64(p.Length), //nolint:mnd
69: 		highBuf:  make([]float64, windowSize),
70: 		lowBuf:   make([]float64, windowSize),
71: 		up:       math.NaN(),
72: 		down:     math.NaN(),
73: 		osc:      math.NaN(),
74: 		mnemonic: mnemonic,
75: 	}, nil
76: }
77: 
78: // IsPrimed indicates whether the indicator is primed.
79: func (a *Aroon) IsPrimed() bool {
80: 	a.mu.RLock()
81: 	defer a.mu.RUnlock()
82: 
83: 	return a.primed
84: }
85: 
86: // Metadata describes the output data of the indicator.
87: func (a *Aroon) Metadata() core.Metadata {
88: 	desc := "Aroon " + a.mnemonic
89: 
90: 	return core.Metadata{
91: 		Type:        core.Aroon,
92: 		Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic,
93: 		Description: desc,
94: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
95: 			{
96: 				Kind:        int(AroonUp),
97: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
98: 				Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic + " up",
99: 				Description: desc + " Up",
100: 			},
101: 			{
102: 				Kind:        int(AroonDown),
103: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
104: 				Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic + " down",
105: 				Description: desc + " Down",
106: 			},
107: 			{
108: 				Kind:        int(AroonOsc),
109: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
110: 				Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic + " osc",
111: 				Description: desc + " Oscillator",
112: 			},
113: 		},
114: 	}
115: }
116: 
117: // Update updates the indicator given the next bar's high and low values.
118: // Returns AroonUp, AroonDown, and AroonOsc.
119: func (a *Aroon) Update(high, low float64) (float64, float64, float64) {
120: 	if math.IsNaN(high) || math.IsNaN(low) {
121: 		return math.NaN(), math.NaN(), math.NaN()
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	a.mu.Lock()
125: 	defer a.mu.Unlock()
126: 
127: 	windowSize := a.length + 1
128: 	today := a.count
129: 
130: 	// Store in circular buffer.
131: 	pos := a.bufIdx
132: 	a.highBuf[pos] = high
133: 	a.lowBuf[pos] = low
134: 	a.bufIdx = (a.bufIdx + 1) % windowSize
135: 	a.count++
136: 
137: 	// Need at least length+1 bars (indices 0..length).
138: 	if a.count < windowSize {
139: 		return a.up, a.down, a.osc
140: 	}
141: 
142: 	trailingIdx := today - a.length
143: 
144: 	if a.count == windowSize {
145: 		// First time: scan entire window to find highest/lowest.
146: 		a.highestIdx = trailingIdx
147: 		a.lowestIdx = trailingIdx
148: 
149: 		for i := trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++ {
150: 			bufPos := i % windowSize
151: 
152: 			if a.highBuf[bufPos] >= a.highBuf[a.highestIdx%windowSize] {
153: 				a.highestIdx = i
154: 			}
155: 
156: 			if a.lowBuf[bufPos] <= a.lowBuf[a.lowestIdx%windowSize] {
157: 				a.lowestIdx = i
158: 			}
159: 		}
160: 	} else {
161: 		// Subsequent: optimized update.
162: 		// Check if tracked indices fell out of window.
163: 		if a.highestIdx < trailingIdx {
164: 			a.highestIdx = trailingIdx
165: 
166: 			for i := trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++ {
167: 				bufPos := i % windowSize
168: 				if a.highBuf[bufPos] >= a.highBuf[a.highestIdx%windowSize] {
169: 					a.highestIdx = i
170: 				}
171: 			}
172: 		} else if high >= a.highBuf[a.highestIdx%windowSize] {
173: 			a.highestIdx = today
174: 		}
175: 
176: 		if a.lowestIdx < trailingIdx {
177: 			a.lowestIdx = trailingIdx
178: 
179: 			for i := trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++ {
180: 				bufPos := i % windowSize
181: 				if a.lowBuf[bufPos] <= a.lowBuf[a.lowestIdx%windowSize] {
182: 					a.lowestIdx = i
183: 				}
184: 			}
185: 		} else if low <= a.lowBuf[a.lowestIdx%windowSize] {
186: 			a.lowestIdx = today
187: 		}
188: 	}
189: 
190: 	a.up = a.factor * float64(a.length-(today-a.highestIdx))
191: 	a.down = a.factor * float64(a.length-(today-a.lowestIdx))
192: 	a.osc = a.up - a.down
193: 
194: 	if !a.primed {
195: 		a.primed = true
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	return a.up, a.down, a.osc
199: }
200: 
201: // UpdateScalar updates the indicator given the next scalar sample.
202: func (a *Aroon) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {
203: 	v := sample.Value
204: 	up, down, osc := a.Update(v, v)
205: 
206: 	output := make([]any, 3) //nolint:mnd
207: 	output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: up}
208: 	output[1] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: down}
209: 	output[2] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: osc}
210: 
211: 	return output
212: }
213: 
214: // UpdateBar updates the indicator given the next bar sample.
215: func (a *Aroon) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {
216: 	up, down, osc := a.Update(sample.High, sample.Low)
217: 
218: 	output := make([]any, 3) //nolint:mnd
219: 	output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: up}
220: 	output[1] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: down}
221: 	output[2] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: osc}
222: 
223: 	return output
224: }
225: 
226: // UpdateQuote updates the indicator given the next quote sample.
227: func (a *Aroon) UpdateQuote(sample *entities.Quote) core.Output {
228: 	v := (sample.Bid + sample.Ask) / 2 //nolint:mnd
229: 
230: 	return a.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: v})
231: }
232: 
233: // UpdateTrade updates the indicator given the next trade sample.
234: func (a *Aroon) UpdateTrade(sample *entities.Trade) core.Output {
235: 	return a.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: sample.Price})
236: }

(End of file - total 236 lines)
</content>
```

242: // String implements the Stringer interface.
243: //
244: //nolint:exhaustive,cyclop,funlen
245: func (t Type) String() string {
246: 	switch t {
247: 	case SimpleMovingAverage:
248: 		return simpleMovingAverage
249: 	case WeightedMovingAverage:
250: 		return weightedMovingAverage
251: 	case TriangularMovingAverage:
252: 		return triangularMovingAverage
253: 	case ExponentialMovingAverage:
254: 		return exponentialMovingAverage
255: 	case DoubleExponentialMovingAverage:
256: 		return doubleExponentialMovingAverage
257: 	case TripleExponentialMovingAverage:
258: 		return tripleExponentialMovingAverage
259: 	case T2ExponentialMovingAverage:
260: 		return t2ExponentialMovingAverage
261: 	case T3ExponentialMovingAverage:
262: 		return t3ExponentialMovingAverage
263: 	case KaufmanAdaptiveMovingAverage:
264: 		return kaufmanAdaptiveMovingAverage
265: 	case JurikMovingAverage:
266: 		return jurikMovingAverage
267: 	case MesaAdaptiveMovingAverage:
268: 		return mesaAdaptiveMovingAverage
269: 	case FractalAdaptiveMovingAverage:
270: 		return fractalAdaptiveMovingAverage
271: 	case Momentum:
272: 		return momentum
273: 	case RateOfChange:
274: 		return rateOfChange
275: 	case RateOfChangePercent:
276: 		return rateOfChangePercent
277: 	case RelativeStrengthIndex:
278: 		return relativeStrengthIndex
279: 	case ChandeMomentumOscillator:
280: 		return chandeMomentumOscillator
281: 	case BollingerBands:
282: 		return bollingerBands
283: 	case Variance:
284: 		return variance

286: 		return standardDeviation
287: 	case GoertzelSpectrum:

304: 		return trueRange

360: 		return unknown
361: 	}
362: }
363: 
364: // IsKnown determines if this indicator type is known.
365: func (t Type) IsKnown() bool {
366: 	return t >= SimpleMovingAverage && t < last
367: }
368: 
369: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
370: func (t Type) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
371: 	const (
372: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown indicator type"
373: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
374: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
375: 	)
376: 
377: 	s := t.String()
378: 	if s == unknown {
379: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
383: 	b = append(b, dqc)
384: 	b = append(b, s...)
385: 	b = append(b, dqc)
386: 
387: 	return b, nil
388: }
389: 
390: // UnmarshalJSON implements the Unmarshaler interface.
391: //
392: //nolint:cyclop,funlen
393: func (t *Type) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
394: 	const (
395: 		errFmt = "cannot unmarshal '%s': unknown indicator type"
396: 		dqs    = "\"" // Double quote string.
397: 	)
398: 
399: 	d := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)
400: 	s := string(d)
401: 
402: 	switch s {
403: 	case simpleMovingAverage:
404: 		*t = SimpleMovingAverage
405: 	case weightedMovingAverage:

331: 		bs := []byte(tt.json)
332: 
333: 		var act Type
334: 
335: 		err := act.UnmarshalJSON(bs)
336: 		if err != nil && tt.succeeded {
337: 			t.Errorf("UnmarshalJSON('%v'): expected success '%v', got error %v", tt.json, exp, err)
338: 
339: 			continue
340: 		}
341: 
342: 		if err == nil && !tt.succeeded {
343: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected error, got success", tt.json)
344: 
345: 			continue
346: 		}
347: 
348: 		if exp != act {
349: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.json, exp, act)
350: 		}
351: 	}
352: }

<content>1: import { BarComponent } from '../../../entities/bar-component';
2: import { QuoteComponent } from '../../../entities/quote-component';

40:      * When false (default), the first input value is used directly (Metastock style).
41:      * Only relevant when movingAverageType is EMA.
42:      */
43:     firstIsAverage?: boolean;
44: 
45:     /**
46:      * A component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.

**Output:**
```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/gerald-appel/percentage-price-oscillator/percentage-price-oscillator.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { componentTripleMnemonic } from '../../core/component-triple-mnemonic';
2: import { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';
3: import { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';
4: import { LineIndicator } from '../../core/line-indicator';
5: import { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';
6: import { ExponentialMovingAverage } from '../../common/exponential-moving-average/exponential-moving-average';
7: import { SimpleMovingAverage } from '../../common/simple-moving-average/simple-moving-average';
8: import { PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput } from './percentage-price-oscillator-output';
9: import { PercentagePriceOscillatorParams, MovingAverageType } from './percentage-price-oscillator-params';
10: 
11: /** Interface for an indicator that accepts a scalar and returns a value. */
12: interface LineUpdater {
13:   update(sample: number): number;
14:   isPrimed(): boolean;
15: }
16: 
17: /** Function to calculate mnemonic of a __PercentagePriceOscillator__ indicator. */
18: export const percentagePriceOscillatorMnemonic = (params: PercentagePriceOscillatorParams): string => {
19:   const cm = componentTripleMnemonic(
20:     params.barComponent,
21:     params.quoteComponent,
22:     params.tradeComponent,
23:   );
24: 
25:   const maLabel = params.movingAverageType === MovingAverageType.EMA ? 'EMA' : 'SMA';
26: 
27:   return `ppo(${maLabel}${params.fastLength}/${maLabel}${params.slowLength}${cm})`;
28: };
29: 
30: /**
31:  * PercentagePriceOscillator is Gerald Appel's Percentage Price Oscillator (PPO).
32:  *
33:  * PPO is calculated by subtracting the slower moving average from the faster moving
34:  * average and then dividing the result by the slower moving average, expressed as a percentage.
35:  *
36:  *   PPO = 100 * (fast_ma - slow_ma) / slow_ma
37:  *
38:  * Reference:
39:  *
40:  * Appel, Gerald (2005). Technical Analysis: Power Tools for Active Investors.
41:  */
42: export class PercentagePriceOscillator extends LineIndicator {
43:   private fastMA: LineUpdater;
44:   private slowMA: LineUpdater;
45:   private value: number;
46: 
47:   /**
48:    * Constructs an instance given the parameters.
49:    */
50:   public constructor(params: PercentagePriceOscillatorParams) {
51:     super();
52: 
53:     const fastLength = Math.floor(params.fastLength);
54:     const slowLength = Math.floor(params.slowLength);
55: 
56:     if (fastLength < 2) {
57:       throw new Error('fast length should be greater than 1');
58:     }
59: 
60:     if (slowLength < 2) {
61:       throw new Error('slow length should be greater than 1');
62:     }
63: 
64:     this.mnemonic = percentagePriceOscillatorMnemonic(params);
65:     this.description = 'Percentage Price Oscillator ' + this.mnemonic;
66:     this.barComponent = params.barComponent;
67:     this.quoteComponent = params.quoteComponent;
68:     this.tradeComponent = params.tradeComponent;
69: 
70:     if (params.movingAverageType === MovingAverageType.EMA) {
71:       this.fastMA = new ExponentialMovingAverage({
72:         length: fastLength,
73:         firstIsAverage: params.firstIsAverage ?? false,
74:       });
75:       this.slowMA = new ExponentialMovingAverage({
76:         length: slowLength,
77:         firstIsAverage: params.firstIsAverage ?? false,
78:       });
79:     } else {
80:       this.fastMA = new SimpleMovingAverage({ length: fastLength });
81:       this.slowMA = new SimpleMovingAverage({ length: slowLength });
82:     }
83: 
84:     this.value = Number.NaN;
85:     this.primed = false;
86:   }
87: 
88:   /** Describes the output data of the indicator. */
89:   public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {
90:     return {
91:       type: IndicatorType.PercentagePriceOscillator,
92:       mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
93:       description: this.description,
94:       outputs: [{
95:         kind: PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput.PercentagePriceOscillatorValue,
96:         type: OutputType.Scalar,
97:         mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
98:         description: this.description,
99:       }],
100:     };
101:   }
102: 
103:   /** Updates the value of the indicator given the next sample. */
104:   public update(sample: number): number {
105:     const epsilon = 1e-8;
106: 
107:     if (Number.isNaN(sample)) {
108:       return sample;
109:     }
110: 
111:     const slow = this.slowMA.update(sample);
112:     const fast = this.fastMA.update(sample);
113:     this.primed = this.slowMA.isPrimed() && this.fastMA.isPrimed();
114: 
115:     if (Number.isNaN(fast) || Number.isNaN(slow)) {
116:       this.value = Number.NaN;
117:       return this.value;
118:     }
119: 
120:     if (Math.abs(slow) < epsilon) {
121:       this.value = 0;
122:     } else {
123:       this.value = 100 * (fast - slow) / slow;
124:     }
125: 
126:     return this.value;
127:   }
128: }

(End of file - total 128 lines)
</content>
```

---

## User

---

## Assistant (Compaction · claude-opus-4.6 · 64.0s)



## Goal

Convert TaLib/MBST indicators to zpano Go and TypeScript implementations. Seven indicators are already complete (STOCHRSI, STOCH, AROON, AD, ADOSC, SAR, TRIX). The current task is implementing **Bollinger Bands (BB)** in both Go and TypeScript.

## Instructions

- **Convert indicators to zpano Go and TypeScript** — Go first, then TypeScript.
- **Go indicators** follow a 5-file pattern: params, output, output_test, impl, impl_test.
- **TS indicators** follow a 4-file pattern: params, output, impl, impl.spec.
- Always create `output_test.go` for Go indicators (table-driven tests for String, IsKnown, MarshalJSON, UnmarshalJSON).
- **Registration:** Add indicator type to `go/indicators/core/type.go` and `ts/indicators/core/indicator-type.ts`. Update `type_test.go` — add to all four test tables. **BB is already registered in both Go and TS** — no registration changes needed.
- **Go module root** is `/home/dev/zpano/go/` — run `go test` from there with `./indicators/...` paths.
- **TS tests** run via `npm test` from `/home/dev/zpano/ts/`.
- Copy test data inline, don't read data from file. Use full data range (252 rows), include NaN values.
- Go tests do NOT use testify — they use raw `t.Errorf`/`t.Fatal` and `math.Abs` for delta comparisons.
- Go tests use `//nolint:testpackage` and are in the same package as the implementation.
- Entity field names: `Quote` has `Bid`/`Ask` (not `BidPrice`/`AskPrice`), `Trade` has `Price`, `Bar` has `Close`.

### BB-Specific Design Decisions (Agreed Upon)

- **Parameters**: `Length` (default 5, min 2), `UpperMultiplier` (default 2.0), `LowerMultiplier` (default 2.0), `IsUnbiased` (optional, default true — matches existing StdDev/Variance convention), `MovingAverageType` (SMA default, EMA option), `FirstIsAverage` (for EMA seeding), plus standard Bar/Quote/Trade components.
- **IsUnbiased as optional**: In Go use `*bool` (nil = default true), in TS use `boolean?` (undefined = true).
- **Always use MA** for the middle band (TaLib approach, not MBST raw-sample approach). MA length = StdDev length.
- **MovingAverageType** is defined locally per-indicator (not shared), pattern: `type MovingAverageType int; const (SMA MovingAverageType = iota; EMA)` in Go, `enum MovingAverageType { SMA = 0, EMA = 1 }` in TS.

### BB Outputs (6 total)

| Output | Type | Go const | String |
|--------|------|----------|--------|
| LowerValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsLowerValue` | `"lowerValue"` |
| MiddleValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsMiddleValue` | `"middleValue"` |
| UpperValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsUpperValue` | `"upperValue"` |
| BandWidth | Scalar | `BollingerBandsBandWidth` | `"bandWidth"` |
| PercentBand | Scalar | `BollingerBandsPercentBand` | `"percentBand"` |
| Band | Band (outputs.BandType) | `BollingerBandsBand` | `"band"` |

### BB Algorithm

```
1. Feed sample to MA(length) → middleValue
2. Feed sample to StdDev(length, isUnbiased) → stddev
3. upperValue = middleValue + upperMultiplier * stddev
4. lowerValue = middleValue - lowerMultiplier * stddev
5. bandWidth = (upperValue - lowerValue) / middleValue  (if middleValue != 0)
6. percentBand = (sample - lowerValue) / (upperValue - lowerValue)  (if upper != lower)
7. Primed when both MA and StdDev are primed
```

Lookback: `length - 1` (same as MA and StdDev with same length).

### BB Test Data

MBST tests use **length=20, multiplier=2** with both **sample (unbiased=true)** and **population (unbiased=false)** stddev variants. The full 252-element test data arrays are in `BollingerBandsTests.cs` — all arrays have been read and are available:
- `ClosingPrice` (252 values)
- `Sma20Expected` (NaN for first 19, then SMA(20) values)
- `SampleStDev20Expected` / `PopulationStDev20Expected`
- `SampleLowerBandExpected` / `PopulationLowerBandExpected`
- `SampleUpperBandExpected` / `PopulationUpperBandExpected`
- `SampleBandWidthExpected` / `PopulationBandWidthExpected`
- `SamplePercentBandExpected` / `PopulationPercentBandExpected`

MBST tests compare at **9 decimal places** precision. The tests verify: NaN for indices 0-18, computed values for indices 19-251.

## Discoveries

- **BollingerBands is already registered** in `go/indicators/core/type.go` (line 64, enum value 18) and `ts/indicators/core/indicator-type.ts` (line 56). Also already in `type_test.go` in all 4 test tables. **No registration changes needed.**
- The existing `StandardDeviation` indicator (Go: `go/indicators/common/standarddeviation/`, TS: `ts/indicators/common/standard-deviation/`) wraps `Variance` and can be used as a sub-component. It takes `Length` and `IsUnbiased` params.
- The existing `Variance` indicator uses a moving window approach tracking `windowSum` and `windowSquaredSum`. Population: `(∑x² - (∑x)²/ℓ) / ℓ`. Sample: `(∑x² - (∑x)²/ℓ) / (ℓ-1)`.
- The `Band` output type already exists: Go `outputs.Band{Time, Lower, Upper}` with `NewBand()` and `NewEmptyBand()` constructors; TS `Band{time, lower, upper}`. Output type constant: `outputs.BandType` (Go) / `OutputType.Band` (TS).
- BB is a **multi-output indicator** similar to Aroon (which returns 3 scalars). BB returns 5 scalars + 1 band = 6 outputs. It does NOT use `LineIndicator` embedding because `Update` returns multiple values. Follow the Aroon pattern for multi-output: custom `UpdateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade` methods returning `core.Output` (slice of `any`).
- The `MovingAverageType` pattern is defined locally in each indicator package that needs it (PPO, STOCHRSI, ADOSC, APO, STOCH). Each has its own `SMA`/`EMA` consts.
- MBST implementation uses raw sample as middle value (no MA smoothing). TaLib always uses MA. We chose **always MA** approach.
- MBST test data uses SMA(20) for middle band expected values (`Sma20Expected`), which matches our always-MA approach.

## Accomplished

### ✅ Previously completed (7 indicators)
- STOCHRSI, STOCH, AROON, AD, ADOSC, SAR, TRIX — all fully complete in Go and TS with passing tests.

3: import "zpano/entities"

5: // MovingAverageType specifies the type of moving average to use in the PPO calculation.
6: type MovingAverageType int
7: 
8: const (
9: 	// SMA uses a Simple Moving Average.
10: 	SMA MovingAverageType = iota
11: 
12: 	// EMA uses an Exponential Moving Average.

14: )
15: 
16: // PercentagePriceOscillatorParams describes parameters to create an instance of the indicator.
17: type PercentagePriceOscillatorParams struct {
18: 	// FastLength is the number of periods for the fast moving average.
19: 	//
20: 	// The value should be greater than 1.
21: 	FastLength int
22: 
23: 	// SlowLength is the number of periods for the slow moving average.
24: 	//
25: 	// The value should be greater than 1.
26: 	SlowLength int
27: 
28: 	// MovingAverageType specifies the type of moving average (SMA or EMA).
29: 	//
30: 	// If zero (SMA), the Simple Moving Average is used.
31: 	MovingAverageType MovingAverageType
32: 
33: 	// FirstIsAverage controls the EMA seeding algorithm.
34: 	// When true, the first EMA value is the simple average of the first period values.
35: 	// When false (default), the first input value is used directly (Metastock style).
36: 	// Only relevant when MovingAverageType is EMA.
37: 	FirstIsAverage bool
38: 
39: 	// BarComponent indicates the component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.
40: 	//
41: 	// If zero, the default (BarClosePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
42: 	BarComponent entities.BarComponent
43: 
44: 	// QuoteComponent indicates the component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample.
45: 	//
46: 	// If zero, the default (QuoteMidPrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.

<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/tusharchande/aroon/aroonoutput.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: //nolint:dupl
2: package aroon
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"bytes"
6: 	"fmt"
7: )
8: 
9: // AroonOutput describes the outputs of the indicator.
10: type AroonOutput int
11: 
12: const (
13: 	// The Aroon Up line.
14: 	AroonUp AroonOutput = iota + 1
15: 
16: 	// The Aroon Down line.
17: 	AroonDown
18: 
19: 	// The Aroon Oscillator (AroonUp - AroonDown).
20: 	AroonOsc
21: 
22: 	aroonLast
23: )
24: 
25: const (
26: 	aroonOutputUp      = "up"
27: 	aroonOutputDown    = "down"
28: 	aroonOutputOsc     = "osc"
29: 	aroonOutputUnknown = "unknown"
30: )
31: 
32: // String implements the Stringer interface.
33: func (o AroonOutput) String() string {
34: 	switch o {
35: 	case AroonUp:
36: 		return aroonOutputUp
37: 	case AroonDown:
38: 		return aroonOutputDown
39: 	case AroonOsc:
40: 		return aroonOutputOsc
41: 	default:
42: 		return aroonOutputUnknown
43: 	}
44: }
45: 
46: // IsKnown determines if this output is known.
47: func (o AroonOutput) IsKnown() bool {
48: 	return o >= AroonUp && o < aroonLast
49: }
50: 
51: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
52: func (o AroonOutput) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
53: 	const (
54: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown aroon output"
55: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
56: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
57: 	)
58: 
59: 	s := o.String()
60: 	if s == aroonOutputUnknown {
61: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
65: 	b = append(b, dqc)
66: 	b = append(b, s...)
67: 	b = append(b, dqc)
68: 
69: 	return b, nil
70: }
71: 
72: // UnmarshalJSON implements the Unmarshaler interface.
73: func (o *AroonOutput) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
74: 	const (
75: 		errFmt = "cannot unmarshal '%s': unknown aroon output"
76: 		dqs    = "\"" // Double quote string.
77: 	)
78: 
79: 	d := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)
80: 	s := string(d)
81: 
82: 	switch s {
83: 	case aroonOutputUp:
84: 		*o = AroonUp
85: 	case aroonOutputDown:
86: 		*o = AroonDown
87: 	case aroonOutputOsc:
88: 		*o = AroonOsc
89: 	default:
90: 		return fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	return nil
94: }

(End of file - total 94 lines)
</content>

**Output:**

<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/tusharchande/aroon/aroon.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package aroon
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"math"
6: 	"sync"
7: 
8: 	"zpano/entities"
9: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
10: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
11: )
12: 
13: // Aroon is Tushar Chande's Aroon indicator.
14: //
15: // The Aroon indicator measures the number of periods since the highest high
16: // and lowest low within a lookback window. It produces three outputs:
17: //   - AroonUp: 100 * (Length - periods since highest high) / Length
18: //   - AroonDown: 100 * (Length - periods since lowest low) / Length
19: //   - AroonOsc: AroonUp - AroonDown
20: //
21: // The indicator requires bar data (high, low). For scalar, quote, and
22: // trade updates, the single value substitutes for both.
23: //
24: // Reference:
25: //
26: // Chande, Tushar S. (1995). "The New Technical Trader". John Wiley & Sons.
27: type Aroon struct {
28: 	mu sync.RWMutex
29: 
30: 	length int
31: 	factor float64
32: 
33: 	// Circular buffers for high and low values (size = length+1).
34: 	highBuf []float64
35: 	lowBuf  []float64
36: 	bufIdx  int
37: 	count   int
38: 
39: 	// Tracked indices of highest high and lowest low (absolute indices).
40: 	highestIdx int
41: 	lowestIdx  int
42: 
43: 	up     float64
44: 	down   float64
45: 	osc    float64
46: 	primed bool
47: 
48: 	mnemonic string
49: }
50: 
51: // NewAroon returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
52: func NewAroon(p *AroonParams) (*Aroon, error) {
53: 	const (
54: 		invalid   = "invalid aroon parameters"
55: 		fmts      = "%s: %s"
56: 		minLength = 2
57: 	)
58: 
59: 	if p.Length < minLength {
60: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "length should be greater than 1")
61: 	}
62: 
63: 	mnemonic := fmt.Sprintf("aroon(%d)", p.Length)
64: 	windowSize := p.Length + 1
65: 
66: 	return &Aroon{
67: 		length:   p.Length,
68: 		factor:   100.0 / float64(p.Length), //nolint:mnd
69: 		highBuf:  make([]float64, windowSize),
70: 		lowBuf:   make([]float64, windowSize),
71: 		up:       math.NaN(),
72: 		down:     math.NaN(),
73: 		osc:      math.NaN(),
74: 		mnemonic: mnemonic,
75: 	}, nil
76: }
77: 
78: // IsPrimed indicates whether the indicator is primed.
79: func (a *Aroon) IsPrimed() bool {
80: 	a.mu.RLock()
81: 	defer a.mu.RUnlock()
82: 
83: 	return a.primed
84: }
85: 
86: // Metadata describes the output data of the indicator.
87: func (a *Aroon) Metadata() core.Metadata {
88: 	desc := "Aroon " + a.mnemonic
89: 
90: 	return core.Metadata{
91: 		Type:        core.Aroon,
92: 		Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic,
93: 		Description: desc,
94: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
95: 			{
96: 				Kind:        int(AroonUp),
97: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
98: 				Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic + " up",
99: 				Description: desc + " Up",
100: 			},
101: 			{
102: 				Kind:        int(AroonDown),
103: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
104: 				Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic + " down",
105: 				Description: desc + " Down",
106: 			},
107: 			{
108: 				Kind:        int(AroonOsc),
109: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
110: 				Mnemonic:    a.mnemonic + " osc",
111: 				Description: desc + " Oscillator",
112: 			},
113: 		},
114: 	}
115: }
116: 
117: // Update updates the indicator given the next bar's high and low values.
118: // Returns AroonUp, AroonDown, and AroonOsc.
119: func (a *Aroon) Update(high, low float64) (float64, float64, float64) {
120: 	if math.IsNaN(high) || math.IsNaN(low) {
121: 		return math.NaN(), math.NaN(), math.NaN()
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	a.mu.Lock()
125: 	defer a.mu.Unlock()
126: 
127: 	windowSize := a.length + 1
128: 	today := a.count
129: 
130: 	// Store in circular buffer.
131: 	pos := a.bufIdx
132: 	a.highBuf[pos] = high
133: 	a.lowBuf[pos] = low
134: 	a.bufIdx = (a.bufIdx + 1) % windowSize
135: 	a.count++
136: 
137: 	// Need at least length+1 bars (indices 0..length).
138: 	if a.count < windowSize {
139: 		return a.up, a.down, a.osc
140: 	}
141: 
142: 	trailingIdx := today - a.length
143: 
144: 	if a.count == windowSize {
145: 		// First time: scan entire window to find highest/lowest.
146: 		a.highestIdx = trailingIdx
147: 		a.lowestIdx = trailingIdx
148: 
149: 		for i := trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++ {
150: 			bufPos := i % windowSize
151: 
152: 			if a.highBuf[bufPos] >= a.highBuf[a.highestIdx%windowSize] {
153: 				a.highestIdx = i
154: 			}
155: 
156: 			if a.lowBuf[bufPos] <= a.lowBuf[a.lowestIdx%windowSize] {
157: 				a.lowestIdx = i
158: 			}
159: 		}
160: 	} else {
161: 		// Subsequent: optimized update.
162: 		// Check if tracked indices fell out of window.
163: 		if a.highestIdx < trailingIdx {
164: 			a.highestIdx = trailingIdx
165: 
166: 			for i := trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++ {
167: 				bufPos := i % windowSize
168: 				if a.highBuf[bufPos] >= a.highBuf[a.highestIdx%windowSize] {
169: 					a.highestIdx = i
170: 				}
171: 			}
172: 		} else if high >= a.highBuf[a.highestIdx%windowSize] {
173: 			a.highestIdx = today
174: 		}
175: 
176: 		if a.lowestIdx < trailingIdx {
177: 			a.lowestIdx = trailingIdx
178: 
179: 			for i := trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++ {
180: 				bufPos := i % windowSize
181: 				if a.lowBuf[bufPos] <= a.lowBuf[a.lowestIdx%windowSize] {
182: 					a.lowestIdx = i
183: 				}
184: 			}
185: 		} else if low <= a.lowBuf[a.lowestIdx%windowSize] {
186: 			a.lowestIdx = today
187: 		}
188: 	}
189: 
190: 	a.up = a.factor * float64(a.length-(today-a.highestIdx))
191: 	a.down = a.factor * float64(a.length-(today-a.lowestIdx))
192: 	a.osc = a.up - a.down
193: 
194: 	if !a.primed {
195: 		a.primed = true
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	return a.up, a.down, a.osc
199: }
200: 
201: // UpdateScalar updates the indicator given the next scalar sample.
202: func (a *Aroon) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {
203: 	v := sample.Value
204: 	up, down, osc := a.Update(v, v)
205: 
206: 	output := make([]any, 3) //nolint:mnd
207: 	output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: up}
208: 	output[1] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: down}
209: 	output[2] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: osc}
210: 
211: 	return output
212: }
213: 
214: // UpdateBar updates the indicator given the next bar sample.
215: func (a *Aroon) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {
216: 	up, down, osc := a.Update(sample.High, sample.Low)
217: 
218: 	output := make([]any, 3) //nolint:mnd
219: 	output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: up}
220: 	output[1] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: down}
221: 	output[2] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: osc}
222: 
223: 	return output
224: }
225: 
226: // UpdateQuote updates the indicator given the next quote sample.
227: func (a *Aroon) UpdateQuote(sample *entities.Quote) core.Output {
228: 	v := (sample.Bid + sample.Ask) / 2 //nolint:mnd
229: 
230: 	return a.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: v})
231: }
232: 
233: // UpdateTrade updates the indicator given the next trade sample.
234: func (a *Aroon) UpdateTrade(sample *entities.Trade) core.Output {
235: 	return a.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: sample.Price})
236: }

(End of file - total 236 lines)
</content>
```

<content>1: //nolint:testpackage,dupl
2: package aroon
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"testing"
6: )
7: 
8: func TestOutputString(t *testing.T) {
9: 	t.Parallel()
10: 
11: 	tests := []struct {
12: 		o    AroonOutput
13: 		text string
14: 	}{
15: 		{AroonUp, aroonOutputUp},
16: 		{AroonDown, aroonOutputDown},
17: 		{AroonOsc, aroonOutputOsc},
18: 		{aroonLast, aroonOutputUnknown},
19: 		{AroonOutput(0), aroonOutputUnknown},
20: 		{AroonOutput(9999), aroonOutputUnknown},
21: 		{AroonOutput(-9999), aroonOutputUnknown},
22: 	}
23: 
24: 	for _, tt := range tests {
25: 		exp := tt.text
26: 		act := tt.o.String()
27: 
28: 		if exp != act {
29: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.String(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
30: 		}
31: 	}
32: }
33: 
34: func TestOutputIsKnown(t *testing.T) {
35: 	t.Parallel()
36: 
37: 	tests := []struct {
38: 		o       AroonOutput
39: 		boolean bool
40: 	}{
41: 		{AroonUp, true},
42: 		{AroonDown, true},
43: 		{AroonOsc, true},
44: 		{aroonLast, false},
45: 		{AroonOutput(0), false},
46: 		{AroonOutput(9999), false},
47: 		{AroonOutput(-9999), false},
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	for _, tt := range tests {
51: 		exp := tt.boolean
52: 		act := tt.o.IsKnown()
53: 
54: 		if exp != act {
55: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.IsKnown(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
56: 		}
57: 	}
58: }
59: 
60: func TestOutputMarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {
61: 	t.Parallel()
62: 
63: 	const dqs = "\""
64: 
65: 	var nilstr string
66: 	tests := []struct {
67: 		o         AroonOutput
68: 		json      string
69: 		succeeded bool
70: 	}{
71: 		{AroonUp, dqs + aroonOutputUp + dqs, true},
72: 		{AroonDown, dqs + aroonOutputDown + dqs, true},
73: 		{AroonOsc, dqs + aroonOutputOsc + dqs, true},
74: 		{aroonLast, nilstr, false},
75: 		{AroonOutput(9999), nilstr, false},
76: 		{AroonOutput(-9999), nilstr, false},
77: 		{AroonOutput(0), nilstr, false},
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	for _, tt := range tests {
81: 		exp := tt.json
82: 		bs, err := tt.o.MarshalJSON()
83: 
84: 		if err != nil && tt.succeeded {
85: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected success '%v', got error %v", tt.o, exp, err)
86: 			continue
87: 		}
88: 
89: 		if err == nil && !tt.succeeded {
90: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected error, got success", tt.o)
91: 			continue
92: 		}
93: 
94: 		act := string(bs)
95: 		if exp != act {
96: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
97: 		}
98: 	}
99: }
100: 
101: func TestOutputUnmarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {
102: 	t.Parallel()
103: 
104: 	const dqs = "\""
105: 
106: 	var zero AroonOutput
107: 	tests := []struct {
108: 		o         AroonOutput
109: 		json      string
110: 		succeeded bool
111: 	}{

114: 		{AroonOsc, dqs + aroonOutputOsc + dqs, true},
115: 		{zero, dqs + aroonOutputUnknown + dqs, false},
116: 		{zero, dqs + "foobar" + dqs, false},
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	for _, tt := range tests {
120: 		exp := tt.o
121: 		bs := []byte(tt.json)
122: 
123: 		var o AroonOutput
124: 
125: 		err := o.UnmarshalJSON(bs)
126: 		if err != nil && tt.succeeded {
127: 			t.Errorf("UnmarshalJSON('%v'): expected success '%v', got error %v", tt.json, exp, err)
128: 			continue
129: 		}
130: 
131: 		if err == nil && !tt.succeeded {
132: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected error, got success", tt.json)
133: 			continue
134: 		}
135: 
136: 		if exp != o {
137: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.json, exp, o)
138: 		}
139: 	}
140: }

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4.6 · 5.9s)

Now let me read the PPO implementation for the MA switching pattern, and the StandardDeviation for sub-component usage:

**Tool: read**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/geraldappel/percentagepriceoscillator/percentagepriceoscillator.go"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/geraldappel/percentagepriceoscillator/percentagepriceoscillator.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package percentagepriceoscillator
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"math"
6: 	"sync"
7: 
8: 	"zpano/entities"
9: 	"zpano/indicators/common/exponentialmovingaverage"
10: 	"zpano/indicators/common/simplemovingaverage"
11: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
12: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
13: )
14: 
15: // lineUpdater is an interface for indicators that accept a single scalar and return a value.
16: type lineUpdater interface {
17: 	Update(float64) float64
18: 	IsPrimed() bool
19: }
20: 
21: // PercentagePriceOscillator is Gerald Appel's Percentage Price Oscillator (PPO).
22: //
23: // PPO is calculated by subtracting the slower moving average from the faster moving
24: // average and then dividing the result by the slower moving average, expressed as a percentage.
25: //
26: //	PPO = 100 * (fast_ma - slow_ma) / slow_ma
27: //
28: // Reference:
29: //
30: // Appel, Gerald (2005). Technical Analysis: Power Tools for Active Investors.
31: type PercentagePriceOscillator struct {
32: 	mu sync.RWMutex
33: 	core.LineIndicator
34: 	fastMA lineUpdater
35: 	slowMA lineUpdater
36: 	value  float64
37: 	primed bool
38: }
39: 
40: // NewPercentagePriceOscillator returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
41: func NewPercentagePriceOscillator(p *PercentagePriceOscillatorParams) (*PercentagePriceOscillator, error) {
42: 	const (
43: 		invalid   = "invalid percentage price oscillator parameters"
44: 		fmts      = "%s: %s"
45: 		fmtw      = "%s: %w"
46: 		minLength = 2
47: 	)
48: 
49: 	if p.FastLength < minLength {
50: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "fast length should be greater than 1")
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	if p.SlowLength < minLength {
54: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "slow length should be greater than 1")
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	bc := p.BarComponent
58: 	if bc == 0 {
59: 		bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	qc := p.QuoteComponent
63: 	if qc == 0 {
64: 		qc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	tc := p.TradeComponent
68: 	if tc == 0 {
69: 		tc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	var (
73: 		err       error
74: 		barFunc   entities.BarFunc
75: 		quoteFunc entities.QuoteFunc
76: 		tradeFunc entities.TradeFunc
77: 	)
78: 
79: 	if barFunc, err = entities.BarComponentFunc(bc); err != nil {
80: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	if quoteFunc, err = entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc); err != nil {
84: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	if tradeFunc, err = entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc); err != nil {
88: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	var fastMA, slowMA lineUpdater
92: 
93: 	var maLabel string
94: 
95: 	switch p.MovingAverageType {
96: 	case EMA:
97: 		maLabel = "EMA"
98: 
99: 		fast, e := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(
100: 			&exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams{
101: 				Length:         p.FastLength,
102: 				FirstIsAverage: p.FirstIsAverage,
103: 			})
104: 		if e != nil {
105: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
106: 		}
107: 
108: 		slow, e := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(
109: 			&exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams{
110: 				Length:         p.SlowLength,
111: 				FirstIsAverage: p.FirstIsAverage,
112: 			})
113: 		if e != nil {
114: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
115: 		}
116: 
117: 		fastMA = fast
118: 		slowMA = slow
119: 	default:
120: 		maLabel = "SMA"
121: 
122: 		fast, e := simplemovingaverage.NewSimpleMovingAverage(
123: 			&simplemovingaverage.SimpleMovingAverageParams{Length: p.FastLength})
124: 		if e != nil {
125: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
126: 		}
127: 
128: 		slow, e := simplemovingaverage.NewSimpleMovingAverage(
129: 			&simplemovingaverage.SimpleMovingAverageParams{Length: p.SlowLength})
130: 		if e != nil {
131: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)
132: 		}
133: 
134: 		fastMA = fast
135: 		slowMA = slow
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	mnemonic := fmt.Sprintf("ppo(%s%d/%s%d%s)", maLabel, p.FastLength, maLabel, p.SlowLength,
139: 		core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc))
140: 	desc := "Percentage Price Oscillator " + mnemonic
141: 
142: 	ppo := &PercentagePriceOscillator{
143: 		fastMA: fastMA,
144: 		slowMA: slowMA,
145: 		value:  math.NaN(),
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	ppo.LineIndicator = core.NewLineIndicator(mnemonic, desc, barFunc, quoteFunc, tradeFunc, ppo.Update)
149: 
150: 	return ppo, nil
151: }
152: 
153: // IsPrimed indicates whether the indicator is primed.
154: func (p *PercentagePriceOscillator) IsPrimed() bool {
155: 	p.mu.RLock()
156: 	defer p.mu.RUnlock()
157: 
158: 	return p.primed
159: }
160: 
161: // Metadata describes the output data of the indicator.
162: func (p *PercentagePriceOscillator) Metadata() core.Metadata {
163: 	return core.Metadata{
164: 		Type:        core.PercentagePriceOscillator,
165: 		Mnemonic:    p.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
166: 		Description: p.LineIndicator.Description,
167: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
168: 			{
169: 				Kind:        int(PercentagePriceOscillatorValue),
170: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
171: 				Mnemonic:    p.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
172: 				Description: p.LineIndicator.Description,
173: 			},
174: 		},
175: 	}
176: }
177: 
178: // Update updates the value of the indicator given the next sample.
179: func (p *PercentagePriceOscillator) Update(sample float64) float64 {
180: 	const epsilon = 1e-8
181: 
182: 	if math.IsNaN(sample) {
183: 		return sample
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	p.mu.Lock()
187: 	defer p.mu.Unlock()
188: 
189: 	slow := p.slowMA.Update(sample)
190: 	fast := p.fastMA.Update(sample)
191: 	p.primed = p.slowMA.IsPrimed() && p.fastMA.IsPrimed()
192: 
193: 	if math.IsNaN(fast) || math.IsNaN(slow) {
194: 		p.value = math.NaN()
195: 
196: 		return p.value
197: 	}
198: 
199: 	if math.Abs(slow) < epsilon {
200: 		p.value = 0
201: 	} else {
202: 		p.value = 100 * (fast - slow) / slow
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	return p.value
206: }

(End of file - total 206 lines)
</content>

  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/common/standarddeviation/standarddeviation.go"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/common/standarddeviation/standarddeviation.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package standarddeviation
2: 
3: //nolint: gofumpt
4: import (
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"math"
7: 
8: 	"zpano/entities"
9: 	"zpano/indicators/common/variance"
10: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
11: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
12: )
13: 
14: // StandardDeviation computes the standard deviation of the samples within a moving window of length ℓ
15: // as a square root of variance:
16: //
17: //	σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/ℓ
18: //
19: // for the estimation of the population variance, or as:
20: //
21: //	σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/(ℓ-1)
22: //
23: // for the unbiased estimation of the sample variance, i={0,…,ℓ-1}.
24: type StandardDeviation struct {
25: 	core.LineIndicator
26: 	variance *variance.Variance
27: }
28: 
29: // NewStandardDeviation returns an instnce of the StandardDeviation indicator created using supplied parameters.
30: func NewStandardDeviation(p *StandardDeviationParams) (*StandardDeviation, error) {
31: 	const (
32: 		fmtn = "stdev.%c(%d%s)"
33: 	)
34: 
35: 	// Resolve defaults for component functions.
36: 	// A zero value means "use default, don't show in mnemonic".
37: 	bc := p.BarComponent
38: 	if bc == 0 {
39: 		bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
40: 	}
41: 
42: 	qc := p.QuoteComponent
43: 	if qc == 0 {
44: 		qc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent
45: 	}
46: 
47: 	tc := p.TradeComponent
48: 	if tc == 0 {
49: 		tc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	// Create the underlying variance indicator.
53: 	vp := &variance.VarianceParams{
54: 		Length:         p.Length,
55: 		IsUnbiased:     p.IsUnbiased,
56: 		BarComponent:   bc,
57: 		QuoteComponent: qc,
58: 		TradeComponent: tc,
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	v, err := variance.NewVariance(vp)
62: 	if err != nil {
63: 		return nil, err
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	var (
67: 		barFunc   entities.BarFunc
68: 		quoteFunc entities.QuoteFunc
69: 		tradeFunc entities.TradeFunc
70: 	)
71: 
72: 	if barFunc, err = entities.BarComponentFunc(bc); err != nil {
73: 		return nil, err
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	if quoteFunc, err = entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc); err != nil {
77: 		return nil, err
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	if tradeFunc, err = entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc); err != nil {
81: 		return nil, err
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	// Build mnemonic using resolved components — defaults are omitted by ComponentTripleMnemonic.
85: 	var c byte = 'p'
86: 	if p.IsUnbiased {
87: 		c = 's'
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	mnemonic := fmt.Sprintf(fmtn, c, p.Length, core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc))
91: 
92: 	var desc string
93: 	if p.IsUnbiased {
94: 		desc = "Standard deviation based on unbiased estimation of the sample variance " + mnemonic
95: 	} else {
96: 		desc = "Standard deviation based on estimation of the population variance " + mnemonic
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	sd := &StandardDeviation{
100: 		variance: v,
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	sd.LineIndicator = core.NewLineIndicator(mnemonic, desc, barFunc, quoteFunc, tradeFunc, sd.Update)
104: 
105: 	return sd, nil
106: }
107: 
108: // IsPrimed indicates whether an indicator is primed.
109: func (s *StandardDeviation) IsPrimed() bool {
110: 	return s.variance.IsPrimed()
111: }
112: 
113: // Metadata describes an output data of the indicator.
114: func (s *StandardDeviation) Metadata() core.Metadata {
115: 	return core.Metadata{
116: 		Type:        core.StandardDeviation,
117: 		Mnemonic:    s.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
118: 		Description: s.LineIndicator.Description,
119: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
120: 			{
121: 				Kind:        int(StandardDeviationValue),
122: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
123: 				Mnemonic:    s.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
124: 				Description: s.LineIndicator.Description,
125: 			},
126: 		},
127: 	}
128: }
129: 
130: // Update updates the value of the standard deviation given the next sample.
131: //
132: // The indicator is not primed during the first ℓ-1 updates.
133: func (s *StandardDeviation) Update(sample float64) float64 {
134: 	v := s.variance.Update(sample)
135: 	if math.IsNaN(v) {
136: 		return v
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	return math.Sqrt(v)
140: }

(End of file - total 140 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package standarddeviation
2: 
3: import "zpano/entities"
4: 
5: // StandardDeviationParams describes parameters to create an instance of the indicator.
6: type StandardDeviationParams struct {
7: 	// Length is the length (the number of time periods, ℓ) of the moving window to calculate the standard deviation.
8: 	//
9: 	// The value should be greater than 1.
10: 	Length int
11: 
12: 	// IsUnbiased indicates whether the estimate of the standard deviation is the unbiased sample standard deviation
13: 	// or the population standard deviation.
14: 	//
15: 	// When in doubt, use the unbiased sample standard deviation (value is true).
16: 	IsUnbiased bool
17: 
18: 	// BarComponent indicates the component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.
19: 	//
20: 	// If zero, the default (BarClosePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
21: 	BarComponent entities.BarComponent
22: 
23: 	// QuoteComponent indicates the component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample.
24: 	//
25: 	// If zero, the default (QuoteMidPrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
26: 	QuoteComponent entities.QuoteComponent
27: 
28: 	// TradeComponent indicates the component of a trade to use when updating the indicator with a trade sample.
29: 	//
30: 	// If zero, the default (TradePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
31: 	TradeComponent entities.TradeComponent
32: }

<content>1: package variance
2: 
3: //nolint: gofumpt
4: import (
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"math"
7: 	"sync"
8: 
9: 	"zpano/entities"
10: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
11: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
12: )
13: 
14: // Variance computes the variance of the samples within a moving window of length ℓ:
15: //
16: //	σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/ℓ
17: //
18: // for the estimation of the population variance, or as:
19: //
20: //	σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/(ℓ-1)
21: //
22: // for the unbiased estimation of the sample variance, i={0,…,ℓ-1}.
23: type Variance struct {
24: 	mu sync.RWMutex
25: 	core.LineIndicator
26: 	window           []float64
27: 	windowSum        float64
28: 	windowSquaredSum float64
29: 	windowLength     int
30: 	windowCount      int
31: 	lastIndex        int
32: 	primed           bool
33: 	unbiased         bool
34: }
35: 
36: // NewVariance returns an instnce of the Variance indicator created using supplied parameters.
37: func NewVariance(p *VarianceParams) (*Variance, error) {
38: 	const (
39: 		invalid = "invalid variance parameters"
40: 		fmts    = "%s: %s"
41: 		fmtw    = "%s: %w"
42: 		fmtn    = "var.%c(%d%s)"
43: 		minlen  = 2
44: 	)
45: 
46: 	length := p.Length
47: 	if length < minlen {
48: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "length should be greater than 1")
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	// Resolve defaults for component functions.
52: 	// A zero value means "use default, don't show in mnemonic".
53: 	bc := p.BarComponent
54: 	if bc == 0 {
55: 		bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	qc := p.QuoteComponent
59: 	if qc == 0 {
60: 		qc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent
61: 	}
62: 
63: 	tc := p.TradeComponent
64: 	if tc == 0 {
65: 		tc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	var (
69: 		err       error
70: 		barFunc   entities.BarFunc
71: 		quoteFunc entities.QuoteFunc
72: 		tradeFunc entities.TradeFunc
73: 	)
74: 
75: 	if barFunc, err = entities.BarComponentFunc(bc); err != nil {
76: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
77: 	}
78: 
79: 	if quoteFunc, err = entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc); err != nil {
80: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	if tradeFunc, err = entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc); err != nil {
84: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	// Build mnemonic using resolved components — defaults are omitted by ComponentTripleMnemonic.
88: 	var c byte = 'p'
89: 	if p.IsUnbiased {
90: 		c = 's'
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	mnemonic := fmt.Sprintf(fmtn, c, length, core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc))
94: 
95: 	var desc string
96: 	if p.IsUnbiased {
97: 		desc = "Unbiased estimation of the sample variance " + mnemonic
98: 	} else {
99: 		desc = "Estimation of the population variance " + mnemonic
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	v := &Variance{
103: 		window:       make([]float64, length),
104: 		windowLength: length,
105: 		lastIndex:    length - 1,
106: 		unbiased:     p.IsUnbiased,
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	v.LineIndicator = core.NewLineIndicator(mnemonic, desc, barFunc, quoteFunc, tradeFunc, v.Update)
110: 
111: 	return v, nil
112: }
113: 
114: // IsPrimed indicates whether an indicator is primed.
115: func (v *Variance) IsPrimed() bool {
116: 	v.mu.RLock()
117: 	defer v.mu.RUnlock()
118: 
119: 	return v.primed
120: }
121: 
122: // Metadata describes an output data of the indicator.
123: // It always has a single scalar output -- the calculated value of the variance.
124: func (v *Variance) Metadata() core.Metadata {
125: 	return core.Metadata{
126: 		Type:        core.Variance,
127: 		Mnemonic:    v.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
128: 		Description: v.LineIndicator.Description,
129: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
130: 			{
131: 				Kind:        int(VarianceValue),
132: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
133: 				Mnemonic:    v.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,
134: 				Description: v.LineIndicator.Description,
135: 			},
136: 		},
137: 	}
138: }
139: 
140: // Update updates the value of the variance, σ², given the next sample.
141: //
142: // Depending on the isUnbiased, the value is the unbiased sample variance or the population variance.
143: //
144: // The indicator is not primed during the first ℓ-1 updates.
145: //
146: //nolint:funlen
147: func (v *Variance) Update(sample float64) float64 {
148: 	if math.IsNaN(sample) {
149: 		return sample
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	var value float64
153: 
154: 	temp := sample
155: 	wlen := float64(v.windowLength)
156: 
157: 	v.mu.Lock()
158: 	defer v.mu.Unlock()
159: 
160: 	//nolint:nestif
161: 	if v.primed {
162: 		v.windowSum += temp
163: 		temp *= temp
164: 		v.windowSquaredSum += temp
165: 		temp = v.window[0]
166: 		v.windowSum -= temp
167: 		temp *= temp
168: 		v.windowSquaredSum -= temp
169: 
170: 		if v.unbiased {
171: 			temp = v.windowSum
172: 			temp *= temp
173: 			temp /= wlen
174: 			value = v.windowSquaredSum - temp
175: 			value /= float64(v.lastIndex)
176: 		} else {
177: 			temp = v.windowSum / wlen
178: 			temp *= temp
179: 			value = v.windowSquaredSum/wlen - temp
180: 		}
181: 
182: 		for i := 0; i < v.lastIndex; i++ {
183: 			v.window[i] = v.window[i+1]
184: 		}
185: 
186: 		v.window[v.lastIndex] = sample
187: 	} else {
188: 		v.windowSum += temp
189: 		v.window[v.windowCount] = temp
190: 		temp *= temp
191: 		v.windowSquaredSum += temp
192: 
193: 		v.windowCount++
194: 		if v.windowLength == v.windowCount {
195: 			v.primed = true
196: 			if v.unbiased {
197: 				temp = v.windowSum
198: 				temp *= temp
199: 				temp /= wlen
200: 				value = v.windowSquaredSum - temp
201: 				value /= float64(v.lastIndex)
202: 			} else {
203: 				temp = v.windowSum / wlen
204: 				temp *= temp
205: 				value = v.windowSquaredSum/wlen - temp
206: 			}
207: 		} else {
208: 			return math.NaN()
209: 		}
210: 	}
211: 
212: 	return value
213: }

(End of file - total 213 lines)
</content>
```

2: 
3: //nolint: gofumpt

14: // SimpleMovingAverage computes the simple, or arithmetic, moving average (SMA) by adding the samples
15: // for a number of time periods (length, ℓ) and then dividing this total by the number of time periods.
16: //
17: // In other words, this is an unweighted mean (gives equal weight to each sample) of the previous ℓ samples.
18: //
19: // This implementation updates the value of the SMA incrementally using the formula:
20: //
21: //	SMAᵢ = SMAᵢ₋₁ + (Pᵢ - Pᵢ₋ℓ) / ℓ,
22: //
23: // where ℓ is the length.
24: //
25: // The indicator is not primed during the first ℓ-1 updates.
26: type SimpleMovingAverage struct {
27: 	mu sync.RWMutex
28: 	core.LineIndicator
29: 	window       []float64
30: 	windowSum    float64
31: 	windowLength int
32: 	windowCount  int
33: 	lastIndex    int
34: 	primed       bool
35: }
36: 
37: // NewSimpleMovingAverage returns an instnce of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
38: func NewSimpleMovingAverage(p *SimpleMovingAverageParams) (*SimpleMovingAverage, error) {
39: 	const (
40: 		invalid = "invalid simple moving average parameters"
41: 		fmts    = "%s: %s"
42: 		fmtw    = "%s: %w"
43: 		fmtn    = "sma(%d%s)"
44: 		minlen  = 2
45: 	)
46: 
47: 	length := p.Length
48: 	if length < minlen {
49: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, "length should be greater than 1")
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	// Resolve defaults for component functions.
53: 	// A zero value means "use default, don't show in mnemonic".
54: 	bc := p.BarComponent
55: 	if bc == 0 {
56: 		bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	qc := p.QuoteComponent
60: 	if qc == 0 {

<content>1: package outputs
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"math"
6: 	"time"
7: )
8: 
9: // Band represents two band values and a time stamp.
10: type Band struct {
11: 	// Time is the date and time of this band.
12: 	Time time.Time `json:"time"`
13: 
14: 	// Lower is the lower value of the band.
15: 	Lower float64 `json:"lower"`
16: 
17: 	// Upper is the upper value of the band.
18: 	Upper float64 `json:"upper"`
19: }
20: 
21: // newBand creates a new band.
22: // Both lower and upper values should not be NaN.
23: func NewBand(time time.Time, lower, upper float64) *Band {
24: 	if lower < upper {
25: 		return &Band{
26: 			Time:  time,
27: 			Lower: lower,
28: 			Upper: upper,
29: 		}
30: 	}
31: 
32: 	return &Band{
33: 		Time:  time,
34: 		Lower: upper,
35: 		Upper: lower,
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: // newEmptyBand creates a new empty band.
40: // Both lower and upper values will be equal to NaN.
41: func NewEmptyBand(time time.Time) *Band {
42: 	nan := math.NaN()
43: 
44: 	return &Band{
45: 		Time:  time,
46: 		Lower: nan,
47: 		Upper: nan,
48: 	}
49: }
50: 
51: // IsEmpty indicates whether this band is not initialized.
52: func (b *Band) IsEmpty() bool {
53: 	return math.IsNaN(b.Lower) || math.IsNaN(b.Upper)
54: }
55: 
56: // String implements the Stringer interface.
57: func (b *Band) String() string {
58: 	return fmt.Sprintf("{%s, %f, %f}", b.Time.Format(timeFmt), b.Lower, b.Upper)
59: }
