420:         [TestMethod]
421:         public void BollingerBands_Metadata_ValuesAreCorrect()
422:         {
423:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
424:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
425:             var metadata = target.Metadata;
426: 
427:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorType.BollingerBands, metadata.IndicatorType);
428:             Assert.IsNotNull(metadata.Outputs);
429:             Assert.AreEqual(7, metadata.Outputs.Length);
430: 
431:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.MiddleMovingAverageValue, metadata.Outputs[0].Kind);
432:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[0].Type);
433: 
434:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.StandardDeviationValue, metadata.Outputs[1].Kind);
435:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[1].Type);
436: 
437:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.LowerBandValue, metadata.Outputs[2].Kind);
438:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[2].Type);
439: 
440:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.UpperBandValue, metadata.Outputs[3].Kind);
441:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[3].Type);
442: 
443:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.PercentBandValue, metadata.Outputs[4].Kind);
444:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[4].Type);
445: 
446:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.BandWidthValue, metadata.Outputs[5].Kind);
447:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Scalar, metadata.Outputs[5].Type);
448: 
449:             Assert.AreEqual((int)BollingerBands.OutputKind.LowerUpperBand, metadata.Outputs[6].Kind);
450:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorOutputType.Band, metadata.Outputs[6].Type);
451:         }
452: 
453:         [TestMethod]
454:         public void BollingerBands_Metadata_SampleStDev_NameAndDescriptionAreCorrect()
455:         {
456:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters { StandardDeviationIsUnbiased = true };
457:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
458:             var metadata = target.Metadata;
459: 
460:             Assert.AreEqual(IndicatorType.BollingerBands, metadata.IndicatorType);
461:             Assert.IsNotNull(metadata.Outputs);
462:             Assert.AreEqual(7, metadata.Outputs.Length);
463: 
464:             Assert.AreEqual("ma-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Name);
465:             Assert.AreEqual("Moving Average used by Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Description);
466: 
467:             Assert.AreEqual("stdev-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Name);
468:             Assert.AreEqual("Standard Deviation of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Description);
469: 
470:             Assert.AreEqual("lo-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Name);
471:             Assert.AreEqual("Lower Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Description);
472: 
473:             Assert.AreEqual("up-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Name);
474:             Assert.AreEqual("Upper Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Description);
475: 
476:             Assert.AreEqual("%b(c)-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Name);
477:             Assert.AreEqual("%B(c) of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Description);
478: 
479:             Assert.AreEqual("bw-bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Name);
480:             Assert.AreEqual("Band Width of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Description);
481: 
482:             Assert.AreEqual("bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Name);
483:             Assert.AreEqual("Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.s(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Description);
484:         }
485: 
486:         [TestMethod]
487:         public void BollingerBands_Metadata_PopulationStDev_NameAndDescriptionAreCorrect()
488:         {
489:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
490:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
491:             var metadata = target.Metadata;
492: 
493:             Assert.AreEqual("ma-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Name);
494:             Assert.AreEqual("Moving Average used by Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[0].Description);
495: 
496:             Assert.AreEqual("stdev-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Name);
497:             Assert.AreEqual("Standard Deviation of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[1].Description);
498: 
499:             Assert.AreEqual("lo-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Name);
500:             Assert.AreEqual("Lower Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[2].Description);
501: 
502:             Assert.AreEqual("up-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Name);
503:             Assert.AreEqual("Upper Band of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[3].Description);
504: 
505:             Assert.AreEqual("%b(c)-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Name);
506:             Assert.AreEqual("%B(c) of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[4].Description);
507: 
508:             Assert.AreEqual("bw-bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Name);
509:             Assert.AreEqual("Band Width of Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[5].Description);
510: 
511:             Assert.AreEqual("bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Name);
512:             Assert.AreEqual("Bollinger Bands bb(stdev.p(20,c),2,sma(20,c))", metadata.Outputs[6].Description);
513:         }
514: 
515:         [TestMethod]
516:         public void BollingerBands_Update_PopulationStDev_Single_AllOutputsRequested_ReturnsCorrectValues()
517:         {
518:             IndicatorOutput output;
519:             var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, 1d);
520:             int count = ClosingPrice.Length;
521: 
522:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
523:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
524: 
525:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
526:             {
527:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
528:                 output = target.Update(scalar);
529:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
530:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
531:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
532:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
533:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
534:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
535:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
536:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
537:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
538:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
539:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
540:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
541:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];
542:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.FirstValue));
543:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.SecondValue));
544:             }
545: 
546:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
547:             {
548:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
549:                 output = target.Update(scalar);
550:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
551:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(Sma20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
552:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
553:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationStDev20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
554:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
555:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationLowerBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
556:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
557:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
558:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
559:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationPercentBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
560:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
561:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationBandWidthExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
562:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];
563:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationLowerBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(band.FirstValue, Decimals));
564:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(band.SecondValue, Decimals));
565:             }
566:         }
567: 
568:         [TestMethod]
569:         public void BollingerBands_Update_SampleStDev_Single_AllOutputsRequested_ReturnsCorrectValues()
570:         {
571:             IndicatorOutput output;
572:             var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, 1d);
573:             int count = ClosingPrice.Length;
574: 
575:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters { StandardDeviationIsUnbiased = true };
576:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
577: 
578:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
579:             {
580:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
581:                 output = target.Update(scalar);
582:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
583:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
584:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
585:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
586:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
587:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
588:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
589:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
590:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
591:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
592:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
593:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
594:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];
595:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.FirstValue));
596:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.SecondValue));
597:             }
598: 
599:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
600:             {
601:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
602:                 output = target.Update(scalar);
603:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
604:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(Sma20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
605:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
606:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleStDev20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
607:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
608:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleLowerBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
609:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
610:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
611:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
612:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SamplePercentBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
613:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
614:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleBandWidthExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
615:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];
616:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleLowerBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(band.FirstValue, Decimals));
617:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(band.SecondValue, Decimals));
618:             }
619:         }
620: 
621:         [TestMethod]
622:         public void BollingerBands_Update_PopulationStDev_Collection_AllOutputsRequested_ReturnsCorrectValues()
623:         {
624:             int count = ClosingPrice.Length;
625:             var scalarArray = new Scalar[count];
626:             for (int i = 0; i < count; ++i)
627:             {
628:                 scalarArray[i] = new Scalar { Time = DateTime.UtcNow, Value = ClosingPrice[i] };
629:             }
630: 
631:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
632:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
633: 
634:             var outputs = target.Update(scalarArray).ToList();
635:             Assert.AreEqual(count, outputs.Count);
636: 
637:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
638:             {
639:                 var output = outputs[i];
640:                 var scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
641:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
642:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
643:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
644:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
645:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
646:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
647:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
648:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
649:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
650:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
651:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
652:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];
653:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.FirstValue));
654:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.SecondValue));
655:             }
656: 
657:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
658:             {
659:                 var output = outputs[i];
660:                 var scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
661:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(Sma20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
662:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
663:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationStDev20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
664:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
665:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationLowerBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
666:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
667:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
668:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
669:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationPercentBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
670:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
671:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationBandWidthExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
672:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];
673:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationLowerBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(band.FirstValue, Decimals));
674:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(PopulationUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(band.SecondValue, Decimals));
675:             }
676:         }
677: 
678:         [TestMethod]
679:         public void BollingerBands_Update_SampleStDev_Collection_AllOutputsRequested_ReturnsCorrectValues()
680:         {
681:             int count = ClosingPrice.Length;
682:             var scalarArray = new Scalar[count];
683:             for (int i = 0; i < count; ++i)
684:             {
685:                 scalarArray[i] = new Scalar { Time = DateTime.UtcNow, Value = ClosingPrice[i] };
686:             }
687: 
688:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters { StandardDeviationIsUnbiased = true };
689:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
690: 
691:             var outputs = target.Update(scalarArray).ToList();
692:             Assert.AreEqual(count, outputs.Count);
693: 
694:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
695:             {
696:                 var output = outputs[i];
697:                 var scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
698:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
699:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
700:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
701:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
702:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
703:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
704:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
705:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
706:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
707:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
708:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
709:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];
710:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.FirstValue));
711:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(band.SecondValue));
712:             }
713: 
714:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
715:             {
716:                 var output = outputs[i];
717:                 var scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[0];
718:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(Sma20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
719:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[1];
720:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleStDev20Expected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
721:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[2];
722:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleLowerBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
723:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[3];
724:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
725:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[4];
726:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SamplePercentBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
727:                 scalar = (Scalar)output.Outputs[5];
728:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleBandWidthExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(scalar.Value, Decimals));
729:                 var band = (Band)output.Outputs[6];

731:                 Assert.AreEqual(Math.Round(SampleUpperBandExpected[i], Decimals), Math.Round(band.SecondValue, Decimals));
732:             }
733:         }
734: 
735:         [TestMethod]
736:         public void BollingerBands_Reset_FunctionsCorrectly()
737:         {

832:         [TestMethod]
833:         public void BollingerBands_IsPrimed_FunctionsCorrectly()
834:         {
835:             var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, 1d);
836:             int count = ClosingPrice.Length;
837: 
838:             var parameters = new BollingerBands.Parameters();
839:             var target = new BollingerBands(parameters, new[] { Middle, StDev, Lower, Upper, PercentB, BandWidth, LowerUpper });
840: 
841:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
842:             {
843:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
844:                 target.Update(scalar);
845:                 Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
846:             }
847: 
848:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
849:             {
850:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
851:                 target.Update(scalar);
852:                 Assert.IsTrue(target.IsPrimed);
853:             }
854: 
855:             target.Reset();
856: 
857:             for (int i = 0; i < 19; ++i)
858:             {
859:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
860:                 target.Update(scalar);
861:                 Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
862:             }
863: 
864:             for (int i = 19; i < count; ++i)
865:             {
866:                 scalar.Value = ClosingPrice[i];
867:                 target.Update(scalar);
868:                 Assert.IsTrue(target.IsPrimed);
869:             }
870:         }

  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/johnbollinger/bollingerbands/bollingerbandsparams.go",
  "content": "package bollingerbands\n\nimport \"zpano/entities\"\n\n// MovingAverageType specifies the type of moving average to use in the Bollinger Bands calculation.\ntype MovingAverageType int\n\nconst (\n\t// SMA uses a Simple Moving Average.\n\tSMA MovingAverageType = iota\n\n\t// EMA uses an Exponential Moving Average.\n\tEMA\n)\n\n// BollingerBandsParams describes parameters to create an instance of the indicator.\ntype BollingerBandsParams struct {\n\t// Length is the number of periods for the moving average and standard deviation.\n\t//\n\t// The value should be greater than 1. The default value is 5.\n\tLength int\n\n\t// UpperMultiplier is the number of standard deviations above the middle band.\n\t//\n\t// The default value is 2.0.\n\tUpperMultiplier float64\n\n\t// LowerMultiplier is the number of standard deviations below the middle band.\n\t//\n\t// The default value is 2.0.\n\tLowerMultiplier float64\n\n\t// IsUnbiased indicates whether to use the unbiased sample standard deviation (true)\n\t// or the population standard deviation (false).\n\t//\n\t// If nil, defaults to true (unbiased sample standard deviation).\n\tIsUnbiased *bool\n\n\t// MovingAverageType specifies the type of moving average (SMA or EMA).\n\t//\n\t// If zero (SMA), the Simple Moving Average is used.\n\tMovingAverageType MovingAverageType\n\n\t// FirstIsAverage controls the EMA seeding algorithm.\n\t// When true, the first EMA value is the simple average of the first period values.\n\t// When false (default), the first input value is used directly (Metastock style).\n\t// Only relevant when MovingAverageType is EMA.\n\tFirstIsAverage bool\n\n\t// BarComponent indicates the component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.\n\t//\n\t// If zero, the default (BarClosePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.\n\tBarComponent entities.BarComponent\n\n\t// QuoteComponent indicates the component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample.\n\t//\n\t// If zero, the default (QuoteMidPrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.\n\tQuoteComponent entities.QuoteComponent\n\n\t// TradeComponent indicates the component of a trade to use when updating the indicator with a trade sample.\n\t//\n\t// If zero, the default (TradePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.\n\tTradeComponent entities.TradeComponent\n}\n"
}

(\n\t// BollingerBandsLowerValue is the lower band value.\n\tBollingerBandsLowerValue BollingerBandsOutput = iota + 1\n\n\t// BollingerBandsMiddleValue is the middle band (moving average) value.\n\tBollingerBandsMiddleValue\n\n\t// BollingerBandsUpperValue is the upper band value.\n\tBollingerBandsUpperValue\n\n\t// BollingerBandsBandWidth is the band width value.\n\tBollingerBandsBandWidth\n\n\t// BollingerBandsPercentBand is the percent band (%B) value.\n\tBollingerBandsPercentBand\n\n\t// BollingerBandsBand is the lower/upper band.\n\tBollingerBandsBand\n\n\tbollingerBandsLast\n)\n\nconst (\n\tbollingerBandsOutputLowerValue  = \"lowerValue\"\n\tbollingerBandsOutputMiddleValue = \"middleValue\"\n\tbollingerBandsOutputUpperValue  = \"upperValue\"\n\tbollingerBandsOutputBandWidth   = \"bandWidth\"\n\tbollingerBandsOutputPercentBand = \"percentBand\"\n\tbollingerBandsOutputBand        = \"band\"\n\tbollingerBandsOutputUnknown     = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// String implements the Stringer interface.\nfunc (o BollingerBandsOutput) String() string {\n\tswitch o {\n\tcase BollingerBandsLowerValue:\n\t\treturn bollingerBandsOutputLowerValue\n\tcase BollingerBandsMiddleValue:\n\t\treturn bollingerBandsOutputMiddleValue\n\tcase BollingerBandsUpperValue:\n\t\treturn bollingerBandsOutputUpperValue\n\tcase BollingerBandsBandWidth:\n\t\treturn bollingerBandsOutputBandWidth\n\tcase BollingerBandsPercentBand:\n\t\treturn bollingerBandsOutputPercentBand\n\tcase BollingerBandsBand:\n\t\treturn bollingerBandsOutputBand\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn bollingerBandsOutputUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsKnown determines if this output is known.\nfunc (o BollingerBandsOutput) IsKnown() bool {\n\treturn o >= BollingerBandsLowerValue && o < bollingerBandsLast\n}\n\n// MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.\nfunc (o BollingerBandsOutput) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n\tconst (\n\t\terrFmt = \"cannot marshal '%s': unknown bollinger bands output\"\n\t\textra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.\n\t\tdqc    = '\"' // Double quote character.\n\t)\n\n\ts := o.String()\n\tif s == bollingerBandsOutputUnknown {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)\n\t}\n\n\tb := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)\n\tb = append(b, dqc)\n\tb = append(b, s...)\n\tb = append(b, dqc)\n\n\treturn b, nil\n}\n\n// UnmarshalJSON implements the Unmarshaler interface.\nfunc (o *BollingerBandsOutput) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {\n\tconst (\n\t\terrFmt = \"cannot unmarshal '%s': unknown bollinger bands output\"\n\t\tdqs    = \"\\\"\" // Double quote string.\n\t)\n\n\td := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)\n\ts := string(d)\n\n\tswitch s {\n\tcase bollingerBandsOutputLowerValue:\n\t\t*o = BollingerBandsLowerValue\n\tcase bollingerBandsOutputMiddleValue:\n\t\t*o = BollingerBandsMiddleValue\n\tcase bollingerBandsOutputUpperValue:\n\t\t*o = BollingerBandsUpperValue\n\tcase bollingerBandsOutputBandWidth:\n\t\t*o = BollingerBandsBandWidth\n\tcase bollingerBandsOutputPercentBand:\n\t\t*o = BollingerBandsPercentBand\n\tcase bollingerBandsOutputBand:\n\t\t*o = BollingerBandsBand\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n"

**Tool: write**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "content": "package bollingerbands\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"zpano/entities\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/common/exponentialmovingaverage\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/common/simplemovingaverage\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/common/variance\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/core\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/core/outputs\"\n)\n\n// lineUpdater is an interface for indicators that accept a single scalar and return a value.\ntype lineUpdater interface {\n\tUpdate(float64) float64\n\tIsPrimed() bool\n}\n\n// BollingerBands is John Bollinger's Bollinger Bands indicator.\n//\n// Bollinger Bands consist of a middle band (moving average) and upper/lower bands\n// placed a specified number of standard deviations above and below the middle band.\n//\n// The indicator produces six outputs:\n//   - LowerValue: middleValue - lowerMultiplier * stddev\n//   - MiddleValue: moving average of the input\n//   - UpperValue: middleValue + upperMultiplier * stddev\n//   - BandWidth: (upperValue - lowerValue) / middleValue\n//   - PercentBand: (sample - lowerValue) / (upperValue - lowerValue)\n//   - Band: lower/upper band pair\n//\n// Reference:\n//\n// Bollinger, John (2002). Bollinger on Bollinger Bands. McGraw-Hill.\ntype BollingerBands struct {\n\tmu sync.RWMutex\n\n\tma       lineUpdater\n\tvariance *variance.Variance\n\n\tupperMultiplier float64\n\tlowerMultiplier float64\n\n\tmiddleValue  float64\n\tupperValue   float64\n\tlowerValue   float64\n\tbandWidth    float64\n\tpercentBand  float64\n\tprimed       bool\n\n\tbarFunc   entities.BarFunc\n\tquoteFunc entities.QuoteFunc\n\ttradeFunc entities.TradeFunc\n\n\tmnemonic string\n}\n\n// NewBollingerBands returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.\n//\n//nolint:funlen,cyclop\nfunc NewBollingerBands(p *BollingerBandsParams) (*BollingerBands, error) {\n\tconst (\n\t\tinvalid          = \"invalid bollinger bands parameters\"\n\t\tfmts             = \"%s: %s\"\n\t\tfmtw             = \"%s: %w\"\n\t\tminLength        = 2\n\t\tdefaultLength    = 5\n\t\tdefaultMultipler = 2.0\n\t)\n\n\tlength := p.Length\n\tif length == 0 {\n\t\tlength = defaultLength\n\t}\n\n\tif length < minLength {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, \"length should be greater than 1\")\n\t}\n\n\tupperMult := p.UpperMultiplier\n\tif upperMult == 0 {\n\t\tupperMult = defaultMultipler\n\t}\n\n\tlowerMult := p.LowerMultiplier\n\tif lowerMult == 0 {\n\t\tlowerMult = defaultMultipler\n\t}\n\n\tisUnbiased := true\n\tif p.IsUnbiased != nil {\n\t\tisUnbiased = *p.IsUnbiased\n\t}\n\n\tbc := p.BarComponent\n\tif bc == 0 {\n\t\tbc = entities.DefaultBarComponent\n\t}\n\n\tqc := p.QuoteComponent\n\tif qc == 0 {\n\t\tqc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent\n\t}\n\n\ttc := p.TradeComponent\n\tif tc == 0 {\n\t\ttc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent\n\t}\n\n\tvar (\n\t\terr       error\n\t\tbarFunc   entities.BarFunc\n\t\tquoteFunc entities.QuoteFunc\n\t\ttradeFunc entities.TradeFunc\n\t)\n\n\tif barFunc, err = entities.BarComponentFunc(bc); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tif quoteFunc, err = entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tif tradeFunc, err = entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create variance sub-indicator.\n\tv, err := variance.NewVariance(&variance.VarianceParams{\n\t\tLength:         length,\n\t\tIsUnbiased:     isUnbiased,\n\t\tBarComponent:   bc,\n\t\tQuoteComponent: qc,\n\t\tTradeComponent: tc,\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create moving average sub-indicator.\n\tvar ma lineUpdater\n\n\tvar maLabel string\n\n\tswitch p.MovingAverageType {\n\tcase EMA:\n\t\tmaLabel = \"EMA\"\n\n\t\tema, e := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(\n\t\t\t&exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams{\n\t\t\t\tLength:         length,\n\t\t\t\tFirstIsAverage: p.FirstIsAverage,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\tif e != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tma = ema\n\tdefault:\n\t\tmaLabel = \"SMA\"\n\n\t\tsma, e := simplemovingaverage.NewSimpleMovingAverage(\n\t\t\t&simplemovingaverage.SimpleMovingAverageParams{Length: length})\n\t\tif e != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, e)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tma = sma\n\t}\n\n\t_ = maLabel // Reserved for future mnemonic use.\n\n\tmnemonic := fmt.Sprintf(\"bb(%d,%.0f,%.0f%s)\", length, upperMult, lowerMult,\n\t\tcore.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc))\n\n\treturn &BollingerBands{\n\t\tma:              ma,\n\t\tvariance:        v,\n\t\tupperMultiplier: upperMult,\n\t\tlowerMultiplier: lowerMult,\n\t\tmiddleValue:     math.NaN(),\n\t\tupperValue:      math.NaN(),\n\t\tlowerValue:      math.NaN(),\n\t\tbandWidth:       math.NaN(),\n\t\tpercentBand:     math.NaN(),\n\t\tbarFunc:         barFunc,\n\t\tquoteFunc:       quoteFunc,\n\t\ttradeFunc:       tradeFunc,\n\t\tmnemonic:        mnemonic,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// IsPrimed indicates whether the indicator is primed.\nfunc (b *BollingerBands) IsPrimed() bool {\n\tb.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer b.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\treturn b.primed\n}\n\n// Metadata describes the output data of the indicator.\nfunc (b *BollingerBands) Metadata() core.Metadata {\n\tdesc := \"Bollinger Bands \" + b.mnemonic\n\n\treturn core.Metadata{\n\t\tType:        core.BollingerBands,\n\t\tMnemonic:    b.mnemonic,\n\t\tDescription: desc,\n\t\tOutputs: []outputs.Metadata{\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(BollingerBandsLowerValue),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    b.mnemonic + \" lower\",\n\t\t\t\tDescription: desc + \" Lower\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(BollingerBandsMiddleValue),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    b.mnemonic + \" middle\",\n\t\t\t\tDescription: desc + \" Middle\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(BollingerBandsUpperValue),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    b.mnemonic + \" upper\",\n\t\t\t\tDescription: desc + \" Upper\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(BollingerBandsBandWidth),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    b.mnemonic + \" bandWidth\",\n\t\t\t\tDescription: desc + \" Band Width\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(BollingerBandsPercentBand),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    b.mnemonic + \" percentBand\",\n\t\t\t\tDescription: desc + \" Percent Band\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(BollingerBandsBand),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.BandType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    b.mnemonic + \" band\",\n\t\t\t\tDescription: desc + \" Band\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// Update updates the indicator given the next sample value.\n// Returns lower, middle, upper, bandWidth, percentBand values.\n//\n//nolint:nonamedreturns\nfunc (b *BollingerBands) Update(sample float64) (lower, middle, upper, bw, pctB float64) {\n\tnan := math.NaN()\n\n\tif math.IsNaN(sample) {\n\t\treturn nan, nan, nan, nan, nan\n\t}\n\n\tb.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer b.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tmiddle = b.ma.Update(sample)\n\tv := b.variance.Update(sample)\n\n\tb.primed = b.ma.IsPrimed() && b.variance.IsPrimed()\n\n\tif math.IsNaN(middle) || math.IsNaN(v) {\n\t\tb.middleValue = nan\n\t\tb.upperValue = nan\n\t\tb.lowerValue = nan\n\t\tb.bandWidth = nan\n\t\tb.percentBand = nan\n\n\t\treturn nan, nan, nan, nan, nan\n\t}\n\n\tstddev := math.Sqrt(v)\n\tupper = middle + b.upperMultiplier*stddev\n\tlower = middle - b.lowerMultiplier*stddev\n\n\tconst epsilon = 1e-10\n\n\tif math.Abs(middle) < epsilon {\n\t\tbw = 0\n\t} else {\n\t\tbw = (upper - lower) / middle\n\t}\n\n\tspread := upper - lower\n\tif math.Abs(spread) < epsilon {\n\t\tpctB = 0\n\t} else {\n\t\tpctB = (sample - lower) / spread\n\t}\n\n\tb.middleValue = middle\n\tb.upperValue = upper\n\tb.lowerValue = lower\n\tb.bandWidth = bw\n\tb.percentBand = pctB\n\n\treturn lower, middle, upper, bw, pctB\n}\n\n// UpdateScalar updates the indicator given the next scalar sample.\nfunc (b *BollingerBands) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {\n\tlower, middle, upper, bw, pctB := b.Update(sample.Value)\n\n\tconst outputCount = 6\n\n\toutput := make([]any, outputCount)\n\toutput[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: lower}\n\toutput[1] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: middle}\n\toutput[2] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: upper}\n\toutput[3] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: bw}\n\toutput[4] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: pctB}\n\n\tif math.IsNaN(lower) || math.IsNaN(upper) {\n\t\toutput[5] = outputs.NewEmptyBand(sample.Time)\n\t} else {\n\t\toutput[5] = outputs.NewBand(sample.Time, lower, upper)\n\t}\n\n\treturn output\n}\n\n// UpdateBar updates the indicator given the next bar sample.\nfunc (b *BollingerBands) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {\n\tv := b.barFunc(sample)\n\n\treturn b.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: v})\n}\n\n// UpdateQuote updates the indicator given the next quote sample.\nfunc (b *BollingerBands) UpdateQuote(sample *entities.Quote) core.Output {\n\tv := b.quoteFunc(sample)\n\n\treturn b.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: v})\n}\n\n// UpdateTrade updates the indicator given the next trade sample.\nfunc (b *BollingerBands) UpdateTrade(sample *entities.Trade) core.Output {\n\tv := b.tradeFunc(sample)\n\n\treturn b.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: v})\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/johnbollinger/bollingerbands/bollingerbands.go"
}

6\n\tif len(meta.Outputs) != expectedOutputs {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected %d outputs, got %d\", expectedOutputs, len(meta.Outputs))\n\t}\n\n\tif meta.Outputs[0].Kind != int(BollingerBandsLowerValue) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected output 0 kind %d, got %d\", BollingerBandsLowerValue, meta.Outputs[0].Kind)\n\t}\n\n\tif meta.Outputs[0].Type != outputs.ScalarType {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected scalar output type, got %v\", meta.Outputs[0].Type)\n\t}\n\n\tif meta.Outputs[1].Kind != int(BollingerBandsMiddleValue) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected output 1 kind %d, got %d\", BollingerBandsMiddleValue, meta.Outputs[1].Kind)\n\t}\n\n\tif meta.Outputs[5].Kind != int(BollingerBandsBand) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected output 5 kind %d, got %d\", BollingerBandsBand, meta.Outputs[5].Kind)\n\t}\n\n\tif meta.Outputs[5].Type != outputs.BandType {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected band output type, got %v\", meta.Outputs[5].Type)\n\t}\n}\n\n//nolint:funlen\nfunc TestBollingerBandsUpdateScalar(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tconst tolerance = 1e-8\n\n\tclosingPrice := testClosingPrice()\n\tsma20 := testSma20Expected()\n\texpLower := testSampleLowerBandExpected()\n\texpUpper := testSampleUpperBandExpected()\n\n\tboolTrue := true\n\tind, err := NewBollingerBands(&BollingerBandsParams{\n\t\tLength:     20,\n\t\tIsUnbiased: &boolTrue,\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\ttm := testTime()\n\n\t// Feed first 19 samples — all NaN.\n\tfor i := 0; i < 19; i++ {\n\t\tout := ind.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: tm, Value: closingPrice[i]})\n\n\t\tv := out[0].(entities.Scalar).Value //nolint:forcetypeassert\n\t\tif !math.IsNaN(v) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"[%d] expected NaN lower scalar, got %v\", i, v)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tband := out[5].(*outputs.Band) //nolint:forcetypeassert\n\t\tif !band.IsEmpty() {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"[%d] expected empty band\", i)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Feed index 19 — first primed value.\n\tout := ind.UpdateScalar(&entities.Scalar{Time: tm, Value: closingPrice[19]})\n\n\tlower := out[0].(entities.Scalar).Value  //nolint:forcetypeassert\n\tmiddle := out[1].(entities.Scalar).Value //nolint:forcetypeassert\n\tupper := out[2].(entities.Scalar).Value  //nolint:forcetypeassert\n\n\tif math.Abs(middle-sma20[19]) > tolerance {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"[19] middle: expected %v, got %v\", sma20[19], middle)\n\t}\n\n\tif math.Abs(lower-expLower[19]) > tolerance {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"[19] lower: expected %v, got %v\", expLower[19], lower)\n\t}\n\n\tif math.Abs(upper-expUpper[19]) > tolerance {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"[19] upper: expected %v, got %v\", expUpper[19], upper)\n\t}\n\n\tband := out[5].(*outputs.Band) //nolint:forcetypeassert\n\tif band.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"[19] expected non-empty band\")\n\t}\n\n\tif math.Abs(band.Lower-expLower[19]) > tolerance {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"[19] band.Lower: expected %v, got %v\", expLower[19], band.Lower)\n\t}\n\n\tif math.Abs(band.Upper-expUpper[19]) > tolerance {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"[19] band.Upper: expected %v, got %v\", expUpper[19], band.Upper)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestBollingerBandsInvalidParams(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname   string\n\t\tlength int\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"length too small\", 1},\n\t\t{\"length zero\", 0},\n\t\t{\"length negative\", -1},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\t_, err := NewBollingerBands(&BollingerBandsParams{Length: tt.length})\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tt.name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/johnbollinger/bollingerbands/bollingerbands_test.go"
}
```

<content>1: import { BarComponent } from '../../../entities/bar-component';
2: import { QuoteComponent } from '../../../entities/quote-component';
3: import { TradeComponent } from '../../../entities/trade-component';
4: 
5: /** Specifies the type of moving average to use in the PPO calculation. */
6: export enum MovingAverageType {
7:   /** Simple Moving Average. */
8:   SMA = 0,
9: 
10:   /** Exponential Moving Average. */
11:   EMA = 1,
12: }
13: 
14: /** Describes parameters to create an instance of the Percentage Price Oscillator indicator. */
15: export interface PercentagePriceOscillatorParams {
16:     /**
17:      * The number of periods for the fast moving average.
18:      *
19:      * The value should be greater than 1.
20:      */
21:     fastLength: number;
22: 
23:     /**
24:      * The number of periods for the slow moving average.
25:      *
26:      * The value should be greater than 1.
27:      */
28:     slowLength: number;
29: 
30:     /**
31:      * The type of moving average (SMA or EMA).
32:      *
33:      * If _undefined_, the Simple Moving Average is used.
34:      */
35:     movingAverageType?: MovingAverageType;
36: 
37:     /**
38:      * Controls the EMA seeding algorithm.
39:      * When true, the first EMA value is the simple average of the first period values.
40:      * When false (default), the first input value is used directly (Metastock style).
41:      * Only relevant when movingAverageType is EMA.
42:      */
43:     firstIsAverage?: boolean;
44: 
45:     /**
46:      * A component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.
47:      *
48:      * If _undefined_, the bar component will have a default value (ClosePrice)
49:      * and will not be shown in the indicator mnemonic.
50:      */
51:     barComponent?: BarComponent;
52: 
53:     /**
54:      * A component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample.
55:      *
56:      * If _undefined_, the quote component will have a default value and will not be shown in the indicator mnemonic.

58:     quoteComponent?: QuoteComponent;
59: 
60:     /**

62:      *

66: }

**Tool: read**

  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/gerald-appel/percentage-price-oscillator/percentage-price-oscillator.ts"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/gerald-appel/percentage-price-oscillator/percentage-price-oscillator.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { componentTripleMnemonic } from '../../core/component-triple-mnemonic';
2: import { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';
3: import { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';
4: import { LineIndicator } from '../../core/line-indicator';
5: import { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';
6: import { ExponentialMovingAverage } from '../../common/exponential-moving-average/exponential-moving-average';
7: import { SimpleMovingAverage } from '../../common/simple-moving-average/simple-moving-average';
8: import { PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput } from './percentage-price-oscillator-output';
9: import { PercentagePriceOscillatorParams, MovingAverageType } from './percentage-price-oscillator-params';
10: 
11: /** Interface for an indicator that accepts a scalar and returns a value. */
12: interface LineUpdater {
13:   update(sample: number): number;
14:   isPrimed(): boolean;
15: }
16: 
17: /** Function to calculate mnemonic of a __PercentagePriceOscillator__ indicator. */
18: export const percentagePriceOscillatorMnemonic = (params: PercentagePriceOscillatorParams): string => {
19:   const cm = componentTripleMnemonic(
20:     params.barComponent,
21:     params.quoteComponent,
22:     params.tradeComponent,
23:   );
24: 
25:   const maLabel = params.movingAverageType === MovingAverageType.EMA ? 'EMA' : 'SMA';
26: 
27:   return `ppo(${maLabel}${params.fastLength}/${maLabel}${params.slowLength}${cm})`;
28: };
29: 
30: /**
31:  * PercentagePriceOscillator is Gerald Appel's Percentage Price Oscillator (PPO).
32:  *
33:  * PPO is calculated by subtracting the slower moving average from the faster moving
34:  * average and then dividing the result by the slower moving average, expressed as a percentage.
35:  *
36:  *   PPO = 100 * (fast_ma - slow_ma) / slow_ma
37:  *
38:  * Reference:
39:  *
40:  * Appel, Gerald (2005). Technical Analysis: Power Tools for Active Investors.
41:  */
42: export class PercentagePriceOscillator extends LineIndicator {
43:   private fastMA: LineUpdater;
44:   private slowMA: LineUpdater;
45:   private value: number;
46: 
47:   /**
48:    * Constructs an instance given the parameters.
49:    */
50:   public constructor(params: PercentagePriceOscillatorParams) {
51:     super();
52: 
53:     const fastLength = Math.floor(params.fastLength);
54:     const slowLength = Math.floor(params.slowLength);
55: 
56:     if (fastLength < 2) {
57:       throw new Error('fast length should be greater than 1');
58:     }
59: 
60:     if (slowLength < 2) {
61:       throw new Error('slow length should be greater than 1');
62:     }
63: 
64:     this.mnemonic = percentagePriceOscillatorMnemonic(params);
65:     this.description = 'Percentage Price Oscillator ' + this.mnemonic;
66:     this.barComponent = params.barComponent;
67:     this.quoteComponent = params.quoteComponent;
68:     this.tradeComponent = params.tradeComponent;
69: 
70:     if (params.movingAverageType === MovingAverageType.EMA) {
71:       this.fastMA = new ExponentialMovingAverage({
72:         length: fastLength,
73:         firstIsAverage: params.firstIsAverage ?? false,
74:       });
75:       this.slowMA = new ExponentialMovingAverage({
76:         length: slowLength,
77:         firstIsAverage: params.firstIsAverage ?? false,
78:       });
79:     } else {
80:       this.fastMA = new SimpleMovingAverage({ length: fastLength });
81:       this.slowMA = new SimpleMovingAverage({ length: slowLength });
82:     }
83: 
84:     this.value = Number.NaN;
85:     this.primed = false;
86:   }
87: 
88:   /** Describes the output data of the indicator. */
89:   public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {
90:     return {
91:       type: IndicatorType.PercentagePriceOscillator,
92:       mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
93:       description: this.description,
94:       outputs: [{
95:         kind: PercentagePriceOscillatorOutput.PercentagePriceOscillatorValue,
96:         type: OutputType.Scalar,
97:         mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
98:         description: this.description,
99:       }],
100:     };
101:   }
102: 
103:   /** Updates the value of the indicator given the next sample. */
104:   public update(sample: number): number {
105:     const epsilon = 1e-8;
106: 
107:     if (Number.isNaN(sample)) {
108:       return sample;
109:     }
110: 
111:     const slow = this.slowMA.update(sample);
112:     const fast = this.fastMA.update(sample);
113:     this.primed = this.slowMA.isPrimed() && this.fastMA.isPrimed();
114: 
115:     if (Number.isNaN(fast) || Number.isNaN(slow)) {
116:       this.value = Number.NaN;
117:       return this.value;
118:     }
119: 
120:     if (Math.abs(slow) < epsilon) {
121:       this.value = 0;
122:     } else {
123:       this.value = 100 * (fast - slow) / slow;
124:     }
125: 
126:     return this.value;
127:   }
128: }

(End of file - total 128 lines)
</content>
```

## Goal

Convert TaLib/MBST indicators to zpano Go and TypeScript implementations. Seven indicators are already complete (STOCHRSI, STOCH, AROON, AD, ADOSC, SAR, TRIX). The current task is implementing **Bollinger Bands (BB)** in both Go and TypeScript.

## Instructions

- **Convert indicators to zpano Go and TypeScript** — Go first, then TypeScript.
- **Go indicators** follow a 5-file pattern: params, output, output_test, impl, impl_test.
- **TS indicators** follow a 4-file pattern: params, output, impl, impl.spec.
- Always create `output_test.go` for Go indicators (table-driven tests for String, IsKnown, MarshalJSON, UnmarshalJSON).
- **Registration:** Add indicator type to `go/indicators/core/type.go` and `ts/indicators/core/indicator-type.ts`. Update `type_test.go` — add to all four test tables. **BB is already registered in both Go and TS** — no registration changes needed.
- **Go module root** is `/home/dev/zpano/go/` — run `go test` from there with `./indicators/...` paths.
- **TS tests** run via `npm test` from `/home/dev/zpano/ts/`.
- Copy test data inline, don't read data from file. Use full data range (252 rows), include NaN values.
- Go tests do NOT use testify — they use raw `t.Errorf`/`t.Fatal` and `math.Abs` for delta comparisons.
- Go tests use `//nolint:testpackage` and are in the same package as the implementation.
- Entity field names: `Quote` has `Bid`/`Ask` (not `BidPrice`/`AskPrice`), `Trade` has `Price`, `Bar` has `Close`.

### BB-Specific Design Decisions (Agreed Upon)

- **Parameters**: `Length` (default 5, min 2), `UpperMultiplier` (default 2.0), `LowerMultiplier` (default 2.0), `IsUnbiased` (optional, default true — matches existing StdDev/Variance convention), `MovingAverageType` (SMA default, EMA option), `FirstIsAverage` (for EMA seeding), plus standard Bar/Quote/Trade components.
- **IsUnbiased as optional**: In Go use `*bool` (nil = default true), in TS use `boolean?` (undefined = true).
- **Always use MA** for the middle band (TaLib approach, not MBST raw-sample approach). MA length = StdDev length.
- **MovingAverageType** is defined locally per-indicator (not shared), pattern: `type MovingAverageType int; const (SMA MovingAverageType = iota; EMA)` in Go, `enum MovingAverageType { SMA = 0, EMA = 1 }` in TS.

### BB Outputs (6 total)

| Output | Type | Go const | String |
|--------|------|----------|--------|
| LowerValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsLowerValue` | `"lowerValue"` |
| MiddleValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsMiddleValue` | `"middleValue"` |
| UpperValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsUpperValue` | `"upperValue"` |
| BandWidth | Scalar | `BollingerBandsBandWidth` | `"bandWidth"` |
| PercentBand | Scalar | `BollingerBandsPercentBand` | `"percentBand"` |
| Band | Band (outputs.BandType) | `BollingerBandsBand` | `"band"` |

### BB Algorithm

```
1. Feed sample to MA(length) → middleValue
2. Feed sample to Variance(length, isUnbiased) → variance, then stddev = sqrt(variance)
3. upperValue = middleValue + upperMultiplier * stddev
4. lowerValue = middleValue - lowerMultiplier * stddev
5. bandWidth = (upperValue - lowerValue) / middleValue  (if middleValue != 0)
6. percentBand = (sample - lowerValue) / (upperValue - lowerValue)  (if upper != lower)
7. Primed when both MA and Variance are primed
```

The Go implementation uses `variance.Variance` directly (not `StandardDeviation` wrapper) and computes `math.Sqrt(v)` inline. The MA is created via the `lineUpdater` interface (same pattern as PPO).

Lookback: `length - 1` (same as MA and StdDev with same length).

### BB Test Data

MBST tests use **length=20, multiplier=2** with both **sample (unbiased=true)** and **population (unbiased=false)** stddev variants. All 252-element test data arrays are in `BollingerBandsTests.cs`. Tests use tolerance of `1e-8`.

## Discoveries

- **BollingerBands is already registered** in `go/indicators/core/type.go` (line 64, enum value 18) and `ts/indicators/core/indicator-type.ts` (line 56). Also already in `type_test.go` in all 4 test tables. **No registration changes needed.**
- The existing `Variance` indicator (`go/indicators/common/variance/`) is used directly as a sub-component. It takes `Length` and `IsUnbiased` params and has an `Update(float64) float64` method.
- The `Band` output type already exists: Go `outputs.Band{Time, Lower, Upper}` with `NewBand()` and `NewEmptyBand()` constructors; TS `Band{time, lower, upper}`. Output type constant: `outputs.BandType` (Go) / `OutputType.Band` (TS).
- BB is a **multi-output indicator** similar to Aroon. It does NOT use `LineIndicator` embedding. It has custom `UpdateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade` methods returning `core.Output` (slice of `any`).
- The `MovingAverageType` pattern is defined locally in each indicator package that needs it (PPO, STOCHRSI, ADOSC, APO, STOCH).
- The Go constructor defaults `Length=0` to 5, so length=0 is NOT an error. The invalid params test only checks length=1 and length=-1.
- Go test data is split into a separate file `testdata_test.go` to keep the test functions file manageable.
- The `EMA` constructor in Go is `exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength` with `ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams{Length, FirstIsAverage}`.
- The `SMA` constructor in Go is `simplemovingaverage.NewSimpleMovingAverage` with `SimpleMovingAverageParams{Length}`.
- Go BB struct stores `barFunc`, `quoteFunc`, `tradeFunc` directly (resolved in constructor via `entities.BarComponentFunc` etc.) rather than using `LineIndicator` embedding.

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4.6 · 6.6s)

**Output:**
```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/tushar-chande/aroon/aroon.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { Bar } from '../../../entities/bar';
2: import { Quote } from '../../../entities/quote';
3: import { Scalar } from '../../../entities/scalar';
4: import { Trade } from '../../../entities/trade';
5: import { Indicator } from '../../core/indicator';
6: import { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';
7: import { IndicatorOutput } from '../../core/indicator-output';
8: import { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';
9: import { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';
10: import { AroonOutput } from './aroon-output';
11: import { AroonParams } from './aroon-params';
12: 
13: /** Function to calculate mnemonic of an __Aroon__ indicator. */
14: export const aroonMnemonic = (params: AroonParams): string => `aroon(${params.length})`;
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Tushar Chande's Aroon indicator.
18:  *
19:  * The Aroon indicator measures the number of periods since the highest high
20:  * and lowest low within a lookback window. It produces three outputs:
21:  *   - AroonUp: 100 * (Length - periods since highest high) / Length
22:  *   - AroonDown: 100 * (Length - periods since lowest low) / Length
23:  *   - AroonOsc: AroonUp - AroonDown
24:  *
25:  * The indicator requires bar data (high, low). For scalar, quote, and
26:  * trade updates, the single value substitutes for both.
27:  *
28:  * Reference:
29:  *
30:  * Chande, Tushar S. (1995). "The New Technical Trader". John Wiley & Sons.
31:  */
32: export class Aroon implements Indicator {
33: 
34:   private readonly length_: number;
35:   private readonly factor: number;
36: 
37:   private readonly highBuf: Float64Array;
38:   private readonly lowBuf: Float64Array;
39:   private bufIdx = 0;
40:   private count = 0;
41: 
42:   private highestIdx = 0;
43:   private lowestIdx = 0;
44: 
45:   private up_ = NaN;
46:   private down_ = NaN;
47:   private osc_ = NaN;
48:   private primed_ = false;
49: 
50:   private readonly mnemonic_: string;
51:   private readonly description_: string;
52: 
53:   constructor(params: AroonParams) {
54:     const length = Math.floor(params.length);
55: 
56:     if (length < 2) {
57:       throw new Error('length should be greater than 1');
58:     }
59: 
60:     this.length_ = length;
61:     this.factor = 100.0 / length;
62: 
63:     const windowSize = length + 1;
64:     this.highBuf = new Float64Array(windowSize);
65:     this.lowBuf = new Float64Array(windowSize);
66: 
67:     this.mnemonic_ = aroonMnemonic(params);
68:     this.description_ = 'Aroon ' + this.mnemonic_;
69:   }
70: 
71:   /** Indicates whether the indicator is primed. */
72:   public isPrimed(): boolean {
73:     return this.primed_;
74:   }
75: 
76:   /** Describes the output data of the indicator. */
77:   public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {
78:     return {
79:       type: IndicatorType.Aroon,
80:       mnemonic: this.mnemonic_,
81:       description: this.description_,
82:       outputs: [
83:         {
84:           kind: AroonOutput.Up,
85:           type: OutputType.Scalar,
86:           mnemonic: this.mnemonic_ + ' up',
87:           description: this.description_ + ' Up',
88:         },
89:         {
90:           kind: AroonOutput.Down,
91:           type: OutputType.Scalar,
92:           mnemonic: this.mnemonic_ + ' down',
93:           description: this.description_ + ' Down',
94:         },
95:         {
96:           kind: AroonOutput.Osc,
97:           type: OutputType.Scalar,
98:           mnemonic: this.mnemonic_ + ' osc',
99:           description: this.description_ + ' Oscillator',
100:         },
101:       ],
102:     };
103:   }
104: 
105:   /** Updates the indicator given the next bar's high and low values. Returns [AroonUp, AroonDown, AroonOsc]. */
106:   public update(high: number, low: number): [number, number, number] {
107:     if (isNaN(high) || isNaN(low)) {
108:       return [NaN, NaN, NaN];
109:     }
110: 
111:     const windowSize = this.length_ + 1;
112:     const today = this.count;
113: 
114:     // Store in circular buffer.
115:     const pos = this.bufIdx;
116:     this.highBuf[pos] = high;
117:     this.lowBuf[pos] = low;
118:     this.bufIdx = (this.bufIdx + 1) % windowSize;
119:     this.count++;
120: 
121:     // Need at least length+1 bars.
122:     if (this.count < windowSize) {
123:       return [this.up_, this.down_, this.osc_];
124:     }
125: 
126:     const trailingIdx = today - this.length_;
127: 
128:     if (this.count === windowSize) {
129:       // First time: scan entire window.
130:       this.highestIdx = trailingIdx;
131:       this.lowestIdx = trailingIdx;
132: 
133:       for (let i = trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++) {
134:         const bufPos = i % windowSize;
135: 
136:         if (this.highBuf[bufPos] >= this.highBuf[this.highestIdx % windowSize]) {
137:           this.highestIdx = i;
138:         }
139: 
140:         if (this.lowBuf[bufPos] <= this.lowBuf[this.lowestIdx % windowSize]) {
141:           this.lowestIdx = i;
142:         }
143:       }
144:     } else {
145:       // Subsequent: optimized update.
146:       if (this.highestIdx < trailingIdx) {
147:         this.highestIdx = trailingIdx;
148: 
149:         for (let i = trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++) {
150:           const bufPos = i % windowSize;
151:           if (this.highBuf[bufPos] >= this.highBuf[this.highestIdx % windowSize]) {
152:             this.highestIdx = i;
153:           }
154:         }
155:       } else if (high >= this.highBuf[this.highestIdx % windowSize]) {
156:         this.highestIdx = today;
157:       }
158: 
159:       if (this.lowestIdx < trailingIdx) {
160:         this.lowestIdx = trailingIdx;
161: 
162:         for (let i = trailingIdx + 1; i <= today; i++) {
163:           const bufPos = i % windowSize;
164:           if (this.lowBuf[bufPos] <= this.lowBuf[this.lowestIdx % windowSize]) {
165:             this.lowestIdx = i;
166:           }
167:         }
168:       } else if (low <= this.lowBuf[this.lowestIdx % windowSize]) {
169:         this.lowestIdx = today;
170:       }
171:     }
172: 
173:     this.up_ = this.factor * (this.length_ - (today - this.highestIdx));
174:     this.down_ = this.factor * (this.length_ - (today - this.lowestIdx));
175:     this.osc_ = this.up_ - this.down_;
176: 
177:     if (!this.primed_) {
178:       this.primed_ = true;
179:     }
180: 
181:     return [this.up_, this.down_, this.osc_];
182:   }
183: 
184:   /** Updates the indicator given the next scalar sample. */
185:   public updateScalar(sample: Scalar): IndicatorOutput {
186:     const v = sample.value;
187:     const [up, down, osc] = this.update(v, v);
188:     const s1 = new Scalar();
189:     s1.time = sample.time;
190:     s1.value = up;
191:     const s2 = new Scalar();
192:     s2.time = sample.time;
193:     s2.value = down;
194:     const s3 = new Scalar();
195:     s3.time = sample.time;
196:     s3.value = osc;
197:     return [s1, s2, s3];
198:   }
199: 
200:   /** Updates the indicator given the next bar sample. */
201:   public updateBar(sample: Bar): IndicatorOutput {
202:     const [up, down, osc] = this.update(sample.high, sample.low);
203:     const s1 = new Scalar();
204:     s1.time = sample.time;
205:     s1.value = up;
206:     const s2 = new Scalar();
207:     s2.time = sample.time;
208:     s2.value = down;
209:     const s3 = new Scalar();
210:     s3.time = sample.time;
211:     s3.value = osc;
212:     return [s1, s2, s3];
213:   }
214: 
215:   /** Updates the indicator given the next quote sample. */
216:   public updateQuote(sample: Quote): IndicatorOutput {
217:     const v = (sample.bid + sample.ask) / 2;
218:     const scalar = new Scalar();
219:     scalar.time = sample.time;
220:     scalar.value = v;
221:     return this.updateScalar(scalar);
222:   }
223: 
224:   /** Updates the indicator given the next trade sample. */
225:   public updateTrade(sample: Trade): IndicatorOutput {
226:     const scalar = new Scalar();
227:     scalar.time = sample.time;
228:     scalar.value = sample.price;
229:     return this.updateScalar(scalar);
230:   }
231: }

(End of file - total 231 lines)
</content>
```

308:   [0.0, 28.571428571428600, -28.571428571428600],

310: ];
311: 
312: describe('Aroon', () => {
313: 
314:   it('should calculate length=14 full data against all 252 expected values', () => {
315:     const tolerance = 1e-6;
316:     const ind = new Aroon({ length: 14 });
317: 
318:     for (let i = 0; i < 252; i++) {
319:       const [up, down, osc] = ind.update(highs[i], lows[i]);
320: 
321:       if (isNaN(expected[i][0])) {
322:         expect(up).withContext(`[${i}] AroonUp`).toBeNaN();
323:         continue;
324:       }
325: 
326:       expect(Math.abs(up - expected[i][0])).withContext(`[${i}] AroonUp`).toBeLessThan(tolerance);
327:       expect(Math.abs(down - expected[i][1])).withContext(`[${i}] AroonDown`).toBeLessThan(tolerance);
328:       expect(Math.abs(osc - expected[i][2])).withContext(`[${i}] AroonOsc`).toBeLessThan(tolerance);
329:     }
330:   });
331: 
332:   it('should report correct primed state', () => {
333:     const ind = new Aroon({ length: 14 });
334: 
335:     expect(ind.isPrimed()).toBe(false);
336: 
337:     for (let i = 0; i < 14; i++) {
338:       ind.update(highs[i], lows[i]);
339:       expect(ind.isPrimed()).withContext(`index ${i}`).toBe(false);
340:     }
341: 
342:     ind.update(highs[14], lows[14]);
343:     expect(ind.isPrimed()).toBe(true);
344:   });
345: 
346:   it('should handle NaN passthrough', () => {
347:     const ind = new Aroon({ length: 14 });
348: 
349:     const [up, down, osc] = ind.update(NaN, 1.0);
350:     expect(up).toBeNaN();
351:     expect(down).toBeNaN();
352:     expect(osc).toBeNaN();
353:   });
354: 
355:   it('should return correct metadata', () => {
356:     const ind = new Aroon({ length: 14 });
357:     const meta = ind.metadata();
358: 
359:     expect(meta.type).toBe(IndicatorType.Aroon);
360:     expect(meta.mnemonic).toBe('aroon(14)');
361:     expect(meta.outputs.length).toBe(3);
362:     expect(meta.outputs[0].kind).toBe(AroonOutput.Up);
363:     expect(meta.outputs[0].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);
364:     expect(meta.outputs[1].kind).toBe(AroonOutput.Down);
365:     expect(meta.outputs[2].kind).toBe(AroonOutput.Osc);
366:   });
367: 
368:   it('should validate parameters', () => {
369:     expect(() => new Aroon({ length: 1 })).toThrow();
370:     expect(() => new Aroon({ length: 0 })).toThrow();
371:     expect(() => new Aroon({ length: -1 })).toThrow();
372:     expect(() => new Aroon({ length: 2 })).not.toThrow();
373:     expect(() => new Aroon({ length: 14 })).not.toThrow();
374:   });
375: });

(End of file - total 375 lines)
</content>

14: /** Describes parameters to create an instance of the Percentage Price Oscillator indicator. */
15: export interface PercentagePriceOscillatorParams {

38:      * Controls the EMA seeding algorithm.
39:      * When true, the first EMA value is the simple average of the first period values.

41:      * Only relevant when movingAverageType is EMA.

```
<path>/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/common/variance/variance.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { componentTripleMnemonic } from '../../core/component-triple-mnemonic';
2: import { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';
3: import { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';
4: import { LineIndicator } from '../../core/line-indicator';
5: import { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';
6: import { VarianceOutput } from './variance-output';
7: import { VarianceParams } from './variance-params';
8: 
9: /** Function to calculate mnemonic of a __Variance__ indicator. */
10: export const varianceMnemonic = (params: VarianceParams): string =>
11:   'var.'.concat(params.unbiased ? 's(' : 'p(', params.length.toString(),
12:   componentTripleMnemonic(params.barComponent, params.quoteComponent, params.tradeComponent), ')');
13: 
14: /** Variance line indicator.
15:  *
16:  * Variance computes the variance of the samples within a moving window of length ℓ:
17:  *
18:  *     σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/ℓ
19:  *
20:  * for the estimation of the population variance, or as:
21:  *
22:  *     σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/(ℓ-1)
23:  *
24:  * for the unbiased estimation of the sample variance, i={0,…,ℓ-1}.
25:  */
26: export class Variance extends LineIndicator {
27:   private window: Array<number>;
28:   private windowLength: number;
29:   private windowSum = 0;
30:   private windowSquaredSum = 0;
31:   private windowCount = 0;
32:   private lastIndex: number;
33:   private unbiased: boolean;
34: 
35:   /**
36:    * Constructs an instance given a length in samples.
37:    * The length should be an integer greater than 1.
38:    **/
39:   public constructor(params: VarianceParams){
40:     super();
41:     const length = Math.floor(params.length);
42:     if (length < 2) {
43:       throw new Error('length should be greater than 1');
44:     }
45: 
46:     this.unbiased = params.unbiased;
47:     this.mnemonic = varianceMnemonic(params);
48:     if (params.unbiased) {
49:       this.description = 'Unbiased estimation of the sample variance ' + this.mnemonic;
50:     } else {
51:       this.description = 'Estimation of the population variance ' + this.mnemonic;
52:     }
53: 
54:     this.barComponent = params.barComponent;
55:     this.quoteComponent = params.quoteComponent;
56:     this.tradeComponent = params.tradeComponent;
57:     this.window = new Array<number>(length);
58:     this.windowLength = length;
59:     this.lastIndex = length - 1;
60:     this.primed = false;
61:   }
62: 
63:   /** Describes the output data of the indicator. */
64:   public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {
65:     return {
66:       type: IndicatorType.Variance,
67:       mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
68:       description: this.description,
69:       outputs: [{
70:         kind: VarianceOutput.VarianceValue,
71:         type: OutputType.Scalar,
72:         mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
73:         description: this.description,
74:       }],
75:     };
76:   }
77: 
78:   /** Updates the value of the indicator given the next sample. */
79:   public update(sample: number): number {
80:     if (Number.isNaN(sample)) {
81:       return sample;
82:     }
83: 
84:     let value = 0;
85:     let temp = sample;
86: 
87:     if (this.primed) {
88:       this.windowSum += temp;
89:       temp *= temp;
90:       this.windowSquaredSum += temp;
91:       temp = this.window[0];
92:       this.windowSum -= temp;
93:       temp *= temp;
94:       this.windowSquaredSum -= temp;
95: 
96:       if (this.unbiased) {
97:         temp = this.windowSum;
98:         temp *= temp;
99:         temp /= this.windowLength;
100:         value = this.windowSquaredSum - temp;
101:         value /= this.lastIndex;
102:       } else {
103:         temp = this.windowSum / this.windowLength;
104:         temp *= temp;
105:         value = this.windowSquaredSum / this.windowLength - temp;
106:       }
107: 
108:       for (let i = 0; i < this.lastIndex; i++) {
109:         this.window[i] = this.window[i+1];
110:       }
111: 
112:       this.window[this.lastIndex] = sample;
113:     } else { // Not primed.
114:       this.windowSum += temp;
115:       this.window[this.windowCount] = temp;
116:       temp *= temp;
117:       this.windowSquaredSum += temp;
118:       this.windowCount++;
119: 
120:       if (this.windowLength > this.windowCount) {
121:         return Number.NaN;
122:       }
123: 
124:       this.primed = true;
125: 
126:       if (this.unbiased) {
127:         temp = this.windowSum;
128:         temp *= temp;
129:         temp /= this.windowLength;
130:         value = this.windowSquaredSum - temp;
131:         value /= this.lastIndex;
132:       } else {
133:         temp = this.windowSum / this.windowLength;
134:         temp *= temp;
135:         value = this.windowSquaredSum / this.windowLength - temp;
136:       }
137:     }
138: 
139:     return value;
140:   }
141: }

(End of file - total 141 lines)
</content>
```

<content>1: package bollingerbands
2: 
3: import "zpano/entities"
4: 
5: // MovingAverageType specifies the type of moving average to use in the Bollinger Bands calculation.
6: type MovingAverageType int
7: 
8: const (
9: 	// SMA uses a Simple Moving Average.
10: 	SMA MovingAverageType = iota
11: 
12: 	// EMA uses an Exponential Moving Average.
13: 	EMA
14: )
15: 
16: // BollingerBandsParams describes parameters to create an instance of the indicator.
17: type BollingerBandsParams struct {
18: 	// Length is the number of periods for the moving average and standard deviation.
19: 	//
20: 	// The value should be greater than 1. The default value is 5.
21: 	Length int
22: 
23: 	// UpperMultiplier is the number of standard deviations above the middle band.
24: 	//
25: 	// The default value is 2.0.
26: 	UpperMultiplier float64
27: 
28: 	// LowerMultiplier is the number of standard deviations below the middle band.
29: 	//
30: 	// The default value is 2.0.
31: 	LowerMultiplier float64
32: 
33: 	// IsUnbiased indicates whether to use the unbiased sample standard deviation (true)
34: 	// or the population standard deviation (false).
35: 	//
36: 	// If nil, defaults to true (unbiased sample standard deviation).
37: 	IsUnbiased *bool
38: 
39: 	// MovingAverageType specifies the type of moving average (SMA or EMA).
40: 	//
41: 	// If zero (SMA), the Simple Moving Average is used.
42: 	MovingAverageType MovingAverageType
43: 
44: 	// FirstIsAverage controls the EMA seeding algorithm.
45: 	// When true, the first EMA value is the simple average of the first period values.
46: 	// When false (default), the first input value is used directly (Metastock style).
47: 	// Only relevant when MovingAverageType is EMA.
48: 	FirstIsAverage bool
49: 
50: 	// BarComponent indicates the component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.
51: 	//
52: 	// If zero, the default (BarClosePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
53: 	BarComponent entities.BarComponent
54: 
55: 	// QuoteComponent indicates the component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample.
56: 	//
57: 	// If zero, the default (QuoteMidPrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
58: 	QuoteComponent entities.QuoteComponent
59: 
60: 	// TradeComponent indicates the component of a trade to use when updating the indicator with a trade sample.
61: 	//
62: 	// If zero, the default (TradePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
63: 	TradeComponent entities.TradeComponent
64: }
